{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01DP3hQijCjkbXBqmEgAXVdv","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Product Requirements Document\n     2→\n     3→## Project Name\n     4→\n     5→Bookmate\n     6→\n     7→## Overview\n     8→\n     9→Bookmate is a web service that helps fiction readers find companions for reading and discussing books together. Users post listings for books they want to read, others join, and once a group forms, they connect via Telegram to coordinate their reading sessions. Think of it as matchmaking for book clubs.\n    10→\n    11→**Target audience:** English-speaking fiction readers who want structured reading companionship but struggle to find like-minded partners.\n    12→\n    13→## Goals\n    14→\n    15→- Make it effortless to find people who want to read the same book at the same pace\n    16→- Remove the friction of organizing a reading group — from discovery to first meeting\n    17→- Keep the platform lightweight: match readers, hand off to Telegram for discussion\n    18→\n    19→## P0 — Must Have (Core, launch blocker)\n    20→\n    21→### User Authentication\n    22→\n    23→Email + password registration and login. Required to post or join any listing.\n    24→\n    25→### Create a Reading Listing\n    26→\n    27→A user creates a listing with:\n    28→- **Book** — selected via Open Library API (title, author, cover art auto-populated)\n    29→- **Preferred language** — language for discussion\n    30→- **Reading pace** — e.g., \"1 chapter per week\", \"50 pages per day\"\n    31→- **Start date** — when the group begins reading\n    32→- **Meeting format** — voice calls, text chat, or mixed\n    33→- **Max group size** — author sets the cap (e.g., 3–6 people)\n    34→\n    35→### Browse Listings\n    36→\n    37→Simple chronological list of open listings. No search or filters at this stage — just a scrollable feed of available reading groups to join.\n    38→\n    39→### Join a Listing\n    40→\n    41→Any authenticated user can join an open listing. First-come-first-served, no approval step. A user can be part of multiple reading groups simultaneously.\n    42→\n    43→### Group Formation\n    44→\n    45→When a listing reaches its max group size:\n    46→- The listing becomes invisible to non-participants (effectively closed)\n    47→- The author is prompted to share a Telegram group link with participants\n    48→- The author manually creates the Telegram group and pastes the invite link\n    49→\n    50→### Notifications\n    51→\n    52→Email + in-app notifications for:\n    53→- **Each new sign-up** — listing author is notified when someone joins\n    54→- **Group full** — all participants are notified when the group reaches its cap\n    55→\n    56→### User Profile (Basic)\n    57→\n    58→- Display name\n    59→- Short bio\n    60→\n    61→## P1 — Should Have (Important, not launch blocker)\n    62→\n    63→### Search and Filters\n    64→\n    65→- Search listings by book title or author\n    66→- Filter by reading pace, start date, meeting format\n    67→- Browsable feed with sorting options\n    68→\n    69→### Telegram Group Creation Assistance\n    70→\n    71→Provide guidance or tooling to help the listing author create and share the Telegram group link more easily (e.g., step-by-step instructions, link validation).\n    72→\n    73→### Extended User Profile\n    74→\n    75→- Reading history (past groups participated in)\n    76→- \"Currently reading\" list\n    77→- Favorite genres\n    78→\n    79→## P2 — Nice to Have (Bonus, if time permits)\n    80→\n    81→### Automatic Telegram Group Creation\n    82→\n    83→System auto-creates a Telegram group via API and shares the invite link with all participants — no manual step needed.\n    84→\n    85→### Applicant Approval System\n    86→\n    87→Listing author can review and approve/reject users who want to join, instead of first-come-first-served.\n    88→\n    89→### Rating and Reputation System\n    90→\n    91→Users can rate their reading group experience. Build a reputation score based on participation history and peer ratings.\n    92→\n    93→### Discord Support\n    94→\n    95→Allow group creation via Discord as an alternative to Telegram.\n    96→\n    97→## Non-Goals\n    98→\n    99→- No payment or monetization features\n   100→- No mobile app (web only)\n   101→- No real-time chat within the platform (groups communicate via Telegram)\n   102→- No complex third-party integrations beyond Open Library API\n   103→- No AI-powered recommendations or matching (AI is allowed where it genuinely improves UX)\n   104→- No content hosting (e-books, PDFs, etc.)\n   105→\n   106→## Tech Stack\n   107→\n   108→- **Frontend + Backend:** Next.js (full-stack)\n   109→- **Database:** SQLite (local, persistent across container restarts)\n   110→- **Analytics:** SQLite-based (no third-party analytics)\n   111→- **Deployment:** Docker container with mounted volume for SQLite persistence\n   112→- **Book data:** Open Library API (free, no API key required)\n   113→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"e8f30eef-be0b-49af-a8d3-712787ead3bb","uuid":"302e0a67-b4b9-40cf-9c58-c57b6cbc49ff","timestamp":"2026-03-23T11:24:50.856Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/PRD.md","content":"# Product Requirements Document\n\n## Project Name\n\nBookmate\n\n## Overview\n\nBookmate is a web service that helps fiction readers find companions for reading and discussing books together. Users post listings for books they want to read, others join, and once a group forms, they connect via Telegram to coordinate their reading sessions. Think of it as matchmaking for book clubs.\n\n**Target audience:** English-speaking fiction readers who want structured reading companionship but struggle to find like-minded partners.\n\n## Goals\n\n- Make it effortless to find people who want to read the same book at the same pace\n- Remove the friction of organizing a reading group — from discovery to first meeting\n- Keep the platform lightweight: match readers, hand off to Telegram for discussion\n\n## P0 — Must Have (Core, launch blocker)\n\n### User Authentication\n\nEmail + password registration and login. Required to post or join any listing.\n\n### Create a Reading Listing\n\nA user creates a listing with:\n- **Book** — selected via Open Library API (title, author, cover art auto-populated)\n- **Preferred language** — language for discussion\n- **Reading pace** — e.g., \"1 chapter per week\", \"50 pages per day\"\n- **Start date** — when the group begins reading\n- **Meeting format** — voice calls, text chat, or mixed\n- **Max group size** — author sets the cap (e.g., 3–6 people)\n\n### Browse Listings\n\nSimple chronological list of open listings. No search or filters at this stage — just a scrollable feed of available reading groups to join.\n\n### Join a Listing\n\nAny authenticated user can join an open listing. First-come-first-served, no approval step. A user can be part of multiple reading groups simultaneously.\n\n### Group Formation\n\nWhen a listing reaches its max group size:\n- The listing becomes invisible to non-participants (effectively closed)\n- The author is prompted to share a Telegram group link with participants\n- The author manually creates the Telegram group and pastes the invite link\n\n### Notifications\n\nEmail + in-app notifications for:\n- **Each new sign-up** — listing author is notified when someone joins\n- **Group full** — all participants are notified when the group reaches its cap\n\n### User Profile (Basic)\n\n- Display name\n- Short bio\n\n## P1 — Should Have (Important, not launch blocker)\n\n### Search and Filters\n\n- Search listings by book title or author\n- Filter by reading pace, start date, meeting format\n- Browsable feed with sorting options\n\n### Telegram Group Creation Assistance\n\nProvide guidance or tooling to help the listing author create and share the Telegram group link more easily (e.g., step-by-step instructions, link validation).\n\n### Extended User Profile\n\n- Reading history (past groups participated in)\n- \"Currently reading\" list\n- Favorite genres\n\n## P2 — Nice to Have (Bonus, if time permits)\n\n### Automatic Telegram Group Creation\n\nSystem auto-creates a Telegram group via API and shares the invite link with all participants — no manual step needed.\n\n### Applicant Approval System\n\nListing author can review and approve/reject users who want to join, instead of first-come-first-served.\n\n### Rating and Reputation System\n\nUsers can rate their reading group experience. Build a reputation score based on participation history and peer ratings.\n\n### Discord Support\n\nAllow group creation via Discord as an alternative to Telegram.\n\n## Non-Goals\n\n- No payment or monetization features\n- No mobile app (web only)\n- No real-time chat within the platform (groups communicate via Telegram)\n- No complex third-party integrations beyond Open Library API\n- No AI-powered recommendations or matching (AI is allowed where it genuinely improves UX)\n- No content hosting (e-books, PDFs, etc.)\n\n## Tech Stack\n\n- **Frontend + Backend:** Next.js (full-stack)\n- **Database:** SQLite (local, persistent across container restarts)\n- **Analytics:** SQLite-based (no third-party analytics)\n- **Deployment:** Docker container with mounted volume for SQLite persistence\n- **Book data:** Open Library API (free, no API key required)\n","numLines":113,"startLine":1,"totalLines":113}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01X68TfKQLxZYtEmNUSgK5k5","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Learned Patterns\n     2→\n     3→## GitHub Authentication\n     4→- Always export GH_TOKEN from .env before running gh commands:\n     5→  ```bash\n     6→  export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2)\n     7→  ```\n     8→- Cannot `--add-reviewer` when token owner is the PR author (HTTP 422)\n     9→- Repo created under TommyBrennan (not XanderZhu — can't create for other users)\n    10→\n    11→## Session Flow\n    12→- First session bootstraps repo, creates issues from PRD as `approved` + `prd`\n    13→- PRD features are pre-approved — no proposal step needed\n    14→- Focus on housekeeping when blocked: organize project board, update memory\n    15→\n    16→## Environment\n    17→- Find agent-browser with: `which agent-browser`\n    18→- agent-browser requires `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp` prefix due to socket dir permissions\n    19→- Playwright chromium needs manual install: `npx playwright install chromium`\n    20→- Git remote uses PAT in URL: `https://ghp_...@github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate.git`\n    21→- Git remote URL may lose PAT between sessions — re-set with `git remote set-url`\n    22→- Must set `git config user.email` and `user.name` each session if not set globally\n    23→\n    24→## Build\n    25→- Next.js 16 Turbopack fails in this environment (can't read /root dir)\n    26→- Use Next.js 15 which defaults to webpack\n    27→- Build from app/ directory: `cd app && npx next build`\n    28→- `useRef<T>()` needs explicit `undefined` arg in React 19: `useRef<T>(undefined)`\n    29→- better-sqlite3 `.all()` returns `unknown[]` — cast explicitly for TS\n    30→\n    31→## Testing\n    32→- Vitest is the test runner: `cd app && npm test`\n    33→- Tests live in `app/__tests__/` with helpers in `app/__tests__/helpers/`\n    34→- Use `vi.hoisted()` with inline `require(\"better-sqlite3\")` to create in-memory DBs for vi.mock factories\n    35→- vi.mock is hoisted above imports — can't reference variables declared after it; use `vi.hoisted()` for shared state\n    36→- Mock bcryptjs to avoid slow hashing (cost factor 12) in unit tests\n    37→- Mock `@/lib/session` to avoid `next/headers` dependency outside request context\n    38→- Route uses `q` param for search (not `search`) and `meeting_format` for format filter (not `format`)\n    39→- ESLint allows `require()` in test files (needed for vi.hoisted pattern)\n    40→- `RequestInit` type needs cast to `NextRequest` for NextRequest constructor compatibility\n    41→\n    42→## Browser Testing\n    43→- agent-browser WORKS with: `export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"`\n    44→- Crashes after extended use (~5+ min) due to Chromium memory pressure\n    45→- Restart between recording scenes: `agent-browser close && rm -rf $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/agent-browser/ && sleep 2`\n    46→- For snapshot timeouts on pages with many elements (e.g., book search dropdown), use `agent-browser screenshot` or JS eval instead\n    47→- Book search results can't use `find text` (multiple matches) — use JS: `document.querySelector(\"button[type=button].w-full\").click()`\n    48→- Reading pace input ref (@e17) times out — use `agent-browser eval` with nativeInputValueSetter to set values\n    49→- For API testing: `curl -s URL | python3 -m json.tool`\n    50→- ffmpeg: static binary at ~/bin/ffmpeg — `export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"` needed each session\n    51→- When recording demos: login user BEFORE starting recording, then navigate to target page\n    52→\n    53→## Deployment\n    54→- No root/sudo access — can't apt-get install, create systemd services, or install nginx\n    55→- PM2 available via npx (not global): `npx pm2 start/stop/restart/list/logs`\n    56→- Deploy script: `./scripts/deploy.sh` (pull, install, build, restart PM2)\n    57→- Production runs on port 3000 directly (no reverse proxy)\n    58→- Public IP: 89.167.127.85 (IPv4), 2a01:4f9:c013:600f::1 (IPv6)\n    59→- Kill stale dev servers before deploy: check `ss -tlnp | grep 3000`\n    60→- PM2 ecosystem config at `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n    61→- PM2 logs at `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n    62→\n    63→## React + SQLite\n    64→- SQLite stores booleans as integers (0/1). In JSX, `{0 && <Component />}` renders \"0\" as text. Always use `!!value` or `Boolean(value)` for numeric fields in `&&` conditionals.\n    65→- Git `add` with bracket directories like `[id]` needs escaping or use from project root\n    66→\n    67→## Security\n    68→- Telegram webhook commands (/link, /link_ID) must verify the sender is the listing author via telegram_user_links table\n    69→- Always validate external URLs before storing (e.g., book_cover_url must start with https://covers.openlibrary.org/)\n    70→- Notification failures should never mask successful DB transactions — wrap in separate try/catch\n    71→- Move related DB writes inside the same transaction for atomicity (e.g., rejection of pending apps when group fills)\n    72→- Query string parameters from URL are always strings — parseInt() before DB queries on integer columns\n    73→\n    74→## Working Directory\n    75→- Project root: /root/Projects/bookmate\n    76→- App directory: /root/Projects/bookmate/app\n    77→- Always use full paths for .claude-beat/.env references\n    78→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"e8f30eef-be0b-49af-a8d3-712787ead3bb","uuid":"1243ef03-caae-4d65-a050-9edfc8dd6935","timestamp":"2026-03-23T11:24:51.399Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md","content":"# Learned Patterns\n\n## GitHub Authentication\n- Always export GH_TOKEN from .env before running gh commands:\n  ```bash\n  export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2)\n  ```\n- Cannot `--add-reviewer` when token owner is the PR author (HTTP 422)\n- Repo created under TommyBrennan (not XanderZhu — can't create for other users)\n\n## Session Flow\n- First session bootstraps repo, creates issues from PRD as `approved` + `prd`\n- PRD features are pre-approved — no proposal step needed\n- Focus on housekeeping when blocked: organize project board, update memory\n\n## Environment\n- Find agent-browser with: `which agent-browser`\n- agent-browser requires `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp` prefix due to socket dir permissions\n- Playwright chromium needs manual install: `npx playwright install chromium`\n- Git remote uses PAT in URL: `https://ghp_...@github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate.git`\n- Git remote URL may lose PAT between sessions — re-set with `git remote set-url`\n- Must set `git config user.email` and `user.name` each session if not set globally\n\n## Build\n- Next.js 16 Turbopack fails in this environment (can't read /root dir)\n- Use Next.js 15 which defaults to webpack\n- Build from app/ directory: `cd app && npx next build`\n- `useRef<T>()` needs explicit `undefined` arg in React 19: `useRef<T>(undefined)`\n- better-sqlite3 `.all()` returns `unknown[]` — cast explicitly for TS\n\n## Testing\n- Vitest is the test runner: `cd app && npm test`\n- Tests live in `app/__tests__/` with helpers in `app/__tests__/helpers/`\n- Use `vi.hoisted()` with inline `require(\"better-sqlite3\")` to create in-memory DBs for vi.mock factories\n- vi.mock is hoisted above imports — can't reference variables declared after it; use `vi.hoisted()` for shared state\n- Mock bcryptjs to avoid slow hashing (cost factor 12) in unit tests\n- Mock `@/lib/session` to avoid `next/headers` dependency outside request context\n- Route uses `q` param for search (not `search`) and `meeting_format` for format filter (not `format`)\n- ESLint allows `require()` in test files (needed for vi.hoisted pattern)\n- `RequestInit` type needs cast to `NextRequest` for NextRequest constructor compatibility\n\n## Browser Testing\n- agent-browser WORKS with: `export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"`\n- Crashes after extended use (~5+ min) due to Chromium memory pressure\n- Restart between recording scenes: `agent-browser close && rm -rf $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/agent-browser/ && sleep 2`\n- For snapshot timeouts on pages with many elements (e.g., book search dropdown), use `agent-browser screenshot` or JS eval instead\n- Book search results can't use `find text` (multiple matches) — use JS: `document.querySelector(\"button[type=button].w-full\").click()`\n- Reading pace input ref (@e17) times out — use `agent-browser eval` with nativeInputValueSetter to set values\n- For API testing: `curl -s URL | python3 -m json.tool`\n- ffmpeg: static binary at ~/bin/ffmpeg — `export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"` needed each session\n- When recording demos: login user BEFORE starting recording, then navigate to target page\n\n## Deployment\n- No root/sudo access — can't apt-get install, create systemd services, or install nginx\n- PM2 available via npx (not global): `npx pm2 start/stop/restart/list/logs`\n- Deploy script: `./scripts/deploy.sh` (pull, install, build, restart PM2)\n- Production runs on port 3000 directly (no reverse proxy)\n- Public IP: 89.167.127.85 (IPv4), 2a01:4f9:c013:600f::1 (IPv6)\n- Kill stale dev servers before deploy: check `ss -tlnp | grep 3000`\n- PM2 ecosystem config at `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n- PM2 logs at `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n\n## React + SQLite\n- SQLite stores booleans as integers (0/1). In JSX, `{0 && <Component />}` renders \"0\" as text. Always use `!!value` or `Boolean(value)` for numeric fields in `&&` conditionals.\n- Git `add` with bracket directories like `[id]` needs escaping or use from project root\n\n## Security\n- Telegram webhook commands (/link, /link_ID) must verify the sender is the listing author via telegram_user_links table\n- Always validate external URLs before storing (e.g., book_cover_url must start with https://covers.openlibrary.org/)\n- Notification failures should never mask successful DB transactions — wrap in separate try/catch\n- Move related DB writes inside the same transaction for atomicity (e.g., rejection of pending apps when group fills)\n- Query string parameters from URL are always strings — parseInt() before DB queries on integer columns\n\n## Working Directory\n- Project root: /root/Projects/bookmate\n- App directory: /root/Projects/bookmate/app\n- Always use full paths for .claude-beat/.env references\n","numLines":78,"startLine":1,"totalLines":78}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01VS7qWDShxLKYND9ekGv7Uc","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Registered Platforms\n     2→\n     3→## GitHub\n     4→- Repo: TommyBrennan/bookmate\n     5→- Project: Bookmate (#1)\n     6→- Auth: GH_TOKEN in .claude-beat/.env\n     7→\n     8→## Production Deployment\n     9→- **Host**: VPS at 89.167.127.85 (IPv6: 2a01:4f9:c013:600f::1)\n    10→- **URL**: http://89.167.127.85:3000\n    11→- **Process manager**: PM2 (via npx, ecosystem.config.js)\n    12→- **Port**: 3000 (direct, no reverse proxy)\n    13→- **Deploy**: `./scripts/deploy.sh`\n    14→- **No HTTPS**: No domain configured, no certbot/nginx (no root access)\n    15→- **DB**: SQLite at app/data/ (persistent on disk)\n    16→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"e8f30eef-be0b-49af-a8d3-712787ead3bb","uuid":"6dd7e772-2a47-426f-9420-157d815d864e","timestamp":"2026-03-23T11:24:51.815Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/memory/platforms.md","content":"# Registered Platforms\n\n## GitHub\n- Repo: TommyBrennan/bookmate\n- Project: Bookmate (#1)\n- Auth: GH_TOKEN in .claude-beat/.env\n\n## Production Deployment\n- **Host**: VPS at 89.167.127.85 (IPv6: 2a01:4f9:c013:600f::1)\n- **URL**: http://89.167.127.85:3000\n- **Process manager**: PM2 (via npx, ecosystem.config.js)\n- **Port**: 3000 (direct, no reverse proxy)\n- **Deploy**: `./scripts/deploy.sh`\n- **No HTTPS**: No domain configured, no certbot/nginx (no root access)\n- **DB**: SQLite at app/data/ (persistent on disk)\n","numLines":16,"startLine":1,"totalLines":16}}}

All approved issues remain closed.\n     5→\n     6→## Review Findings\n     7→- **Production**: Healthy (35min uptime, HTTP 200, DB connected)\n     8→- **CI**: Green on main (all recent runs passing)\n     9→- **PRD sync**: Complete (all PRD features have corresponding closed issues)\n    10→- **Open PRs**: None\n    11→- **Open issues**: #54 (needs-human, PAT expiry ~5 days), #21 (needs-human, Telegram token)\n    12→- **Email**: No actionable items (daily digest, CI notifications, PAT warning already tracked)\n    13→\n    14→## Work Completed\n    15→### Issue #63: PM2 Health Watchdog (PR #64 — merged)\n    16→- Created `scripts/health-watchdog.sh`:\n    17→  - Checks `/api/health` with 10s timeout\n    18→  - 2 retries with 5s backoff before restarting\n    19→  - Logs to `.claude-beat/logs/watchdog.log`\n    20→  - Hourly OK logs to avoid spam, immediate logging on failures\n    21→- Installed cron job: `*/5 * * * *`\n    22→- Tested: script runs, logs healthy status\n    23→- PR created, CI passed, merged\n    24→\n    25→## Issues Monitored\n    26→- **#54 (PAT expiry)**: Still needs-human. ~5 days remaining.\n    27→- **#21 (Telegram token)**: Still needs-human.\n    28→\n    29→## Next Session Priority\n    30→1. Check production health (monitor watchdog logs for any restarts)\n    31→2. Monitor PAT expiry (#54) — ~5 days remaining\n    32→3. Look for new work — all approved issues are closed\n    33→4. Consider proposing: search/filter improvements, UI polish, or performance optimizations\n    34→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"e8f30eef-be0b-49af-a8d3-712787ead3bb","uuid":"499de567-ce90-4d28-a736-d7d827825426","timestamp":"2026-03-23T11:24:58.720Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/logs/session-2026-03-21-1400.md","content":"# Session Log — 2026-03-21 14:00 UTC\n\n## Summary\nRoutine health check session. Production stable. Implemented PM2 health watchdog (issue #63, PR #64) to auto-restart on server hangs. All approved issues remain closed.\n\n## Review Findings\n- **Production**: Healthy (35min uptime, HTTP 200, DB connected)\n- **CI**: Green on main (all recent runs passing)\n- **PRD sync**: Complete (all PRD features have corresponding closed issues)\n- **Open PRs**: None\n- **Open issues**: #54 (needs-human, PAT expiry ~5 days), #21 (needs-human, Telegram token)\n- **Email**: No actionable items (daily digest, CI notifications, PAT warning already tracked)\n\n## Work Completed\n### Issue #63: PM2 Health Watchdog (PR #64 — merged)\n- Created `scripts/health-watchdog.sh`:\n  - Checks `/api/health` with 10s timeout\n  - 2 retries with 5s backoff before restarting\n  - Logs to `.claude-beat/logs/watchdog.log`\n  - Hourly OK logs to avoid spam, immediate logging on failures\n- Installed cron job: `*/5 * * * *`\n- Tested: script runs, logs healthy status\n- PR created, CI passed, merged\n\n## Issues Monitored\n- **#54 (PAT expiry)**: Still needs-human. ~5 days remaining.\n- **#21 (Telegram token)**: Still needs-human.\n\n## Next Session Priority\n1. Check production health (monitor watchdog logs for any restarts)\n2. Monitor PAT expiry (#54) — ~5 days remaining\n3. Look for new work — all approved issues are closed\n4. Match, group up, and discuss on Telegram.\",\n    21→  },\n    22→};\n    23→\n    24→export default function RootLayout({\n    25→  children,\n    26→}: {\n    27→  children: React.ReactNode;\n    28→}) {\n    29→  return (\n    30→    <html lang=\"en\">\n    31→      <body className=\"min-h-screen\">\n    32→        <a href=\"#main-content\" className=\"skip-link\">\n    33→          Skip to content\n    34→        </a>\n    35→        <Navbar />\n    36→        <main id=\"main-content\" className=\"max-w-5xl mx-auto px-3 sm:px-4 py-4 sm:py-8\">{children}</main>\n    37→      </body>\n    38→    </html>\n    39→  );\n    40→}\n    41→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"e8f30eef-be0b-49af-a8d3-712787ead3bb","uuid":"eae1e9d9-1644-4016-94ae-f1d9cdcda70a","timestamp":"2026-03-23T11:25:32.243Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/layout.tsx","content":"import type { Metadata } from \"next\";\nimport \"./globals.css\";\nimport Navbar from \"@/components/Navbar\";\n\nexport const metadata: Metadata = {\n  title: \"Bookmate — Find Your Reading Companion\",\n  description:\n    \"Find people who want to read the same book at the same pace. Match, group up, and discuss on Telegram.\",\n  openGraph: {\n    title: \"Bookmate — Find Your Reading Companion\",\n    description:\n      \"Find people who want to read the same book at the same pace. Match, group up, and discuss on Telegram.\",\n    type: \"website\",\n    siteName: \"Bookmate\",\n  },\n  twitter: {\n    card: \"summary\",\n    title: \"Bookmate — Find Your Reading Companion\",\n    description:\n      \"Find people who want to read the same book at the same pace. {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01XZPJrzGP5PZ2pa2b7XyRuH","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→\"use client\";\n     2→\n     3→import { useEffect, useState, useCallback, useRef } from \"react\";\n     4→import Link from \"next/link\";\n     5→import Image from \"next/image\";\n     6→\n     7→interface Listing {\n     8→  id: number;\n     9→  book_title: string;\n    10→  book_author: string;\n    11→  book_cover_url: string;\n    12→  language: string;\n    13→  reading_pace: string;\n    14→  start_date: string;\n    15→  meeting_format: string;\n    16→  max_group_size: number;\n    17→  requires_approval: number;\n    18→  platform_preference: string;\n    19→  member_count: number;\n    20→  author_name: string;\n    21→  created_at: string;\n    22→}\n    23→\n    24→export default function HomePage() {\n    25→  const [listings, setListings] = useState<Listing[]>([]);\n    26→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);\n    27→  const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null);\n    28→\n    29→  // Search & filter state\n    30→  const [searchQuery, setSearchQuery] = useState(\"\");\n    31→  const [meetingFormat, setMeetingFormat] = useState(\"\");\n    32→  const [readingPace, setReadingPace] = useState(\"\");\n    33→  const [startDateFrom, setStartDateFrom] = useState(\"\");\n    34→  const [sort, setSort] = useState(\"newest\");\n    35→  const [filtersOpen, setFiltersOpen] = useState(false);\n    36→\n    37→  // Pagination state\n    38→  const [page, setPage] = useState(1);\n    39→  const [totalPages, setTotalPages] = useState(1);\n    40→  const [totalResults, setTotalResults] = useState(0);\n    41→\n    42→  const debounceRef = useRef<ReturnType<typeof setTimeout>>(undefined);\n    43→\n    44→  const fetchListings = useCallback(\n    45→    (query: string, format: string, sortBy: string, pace?: string, dateFrom?: string, pageNum?: number) => {\n    46→      setLoading(true);\n    47→      const params = new URLSearchParams();\n    48→      if (query) params.set(\"q\", query);\n    49→      if (format) params.set(\"meeting_format\", format);\n    50→      if (pace) params.set(\"reading_pace\", pace);\n    51→      if (dateFrom) params.set(\"start_date_from\", dateFrom);\n    52→      if (sortBy) params.set(\"sort\", sortBy);\n    53→      if (pageNum && pageNum > 1) params.set(\"page\", String(pageNum));\n    54→\n    55→      const qs = params.toString();\n    56→      fetch(`/api/listings${qs ? `?${qs}` : \"\"}`)\n    57→        .then((r) => {\n    58→          if (!r.ok) throw new Error(\"Failed to load listings\");\n    59→          return r.json();\n    60→        })\n    61→        .then((data) => {\n    62→          setListings(data.listings || []);\n    63→          if (data.pagination) {\n    64→            setTotalPages(data.pagination.totalPages || 1);\n    65→            setTotalResults(data.pagination.total || 0);\n    66→          }\n    67→          setError(null);\n    68→          setLoading(false);\n    69→        })\n    70→        .catch((err) => {\n    71→          setError(err.message || \"Could not load listings. Please try again.\");\n    72→          setLoading(false);\n    73→        });\n    74→    },\n    75→    []\n    76→  );\n    77→\n    78→  // Initial fetch\n    79→  useEffect(() => {\n    80→    fetchListings(\"\", \"\", \"newest\", \"\", \"\", 1);\n    81→  }, [fetchListings]);\n    82→\n    83→  // Debounced search\n    84→  const handleSearchChange = (value: string) => {\n    85→    setSearchQuery(value);\n    86→    setPage(1);\n    87→    if (debounceRef.current) clearTimeout(debounceRef.current);\n    88→    debounceRef.current = setTimeout(() => {\n    89→      fetchListings(value, meetingFormat, sort, readingPace, startDateFrom, 1);\n    90→    }, 400);\n    91→  };\n    92→\n    93→  // Immediate filter changes\n    94→  const handleFormatChange = (value: string) => {\n    95→    setMeetingFormat(value);\n    96→    setPage(1);\n    97→    fetchListings(searchQuery, value, sort, readingPace, startDateFrom, 1);\n    98→  };\n    99→\n   100→  const handlePaceChange = (value: string) => {\n   101→    setReadingPace(value);\n   102→    setPage(1);\n   103→    fetchListings(searchQuery, meetingFormat, sort, value, startDateFrom, 1);\n   104→  };\n   105→\n   106→  const handleStartDateFromChange = (value: string) => {\n   107→    setStartDateFrom(value);\n   108→    setPage(1);\n   109→    fetchListings(searchQuery, meetingFormat, sort, readingPace, value, 1);\n   110→  };\n   111→\n   112→  const handleSortChange = (value: string) => {\n   113→    setSort(value);\n   114→    setPage(1);\n   115→    fetchListings(searchQuery, meetingFormat, value, readingPace, startDateFrom, 1);\n   116→  };\n   117→\n   118→  const handlePageChange = (newPage: number) => {\n   119→    setPage(newPage);\n   120→    fetchListings(searchQuery, meetingFormat, sort, readingPace, startDateFrom, newPage);\n   121→    window.scrollTo({ top: 0, behavior: \"smooth\" });\n   122→  };\n   123→\n   124→  const clearFilters = () => {\n   125→    setSearchQuery(\"\");\n   126→    setMeetingFormat(\"\");\n   127→    setReadingPace(\"\");\n   128→    setStartDateFrom(\"\");\n   129→    setSort(\"newest\");\n   130→    setPage(1);\n   131→    fetchListings(\"\", \"\", \"newest\", \"\", \"\", 1);\n   132→  };\n   133→\n   134→  const hasActiveFilters = searchQuery || meetingFormat || readingPace || startDateFrom || sort !== \"newest\";\n   135→\n   136→  const formatMeetingType = (f: string) => {\n   137→    const labels: Record<string, string> = {\n   138→      voice: \"Voice calls\",\n   139→      text: \"Text chat\",\n   140→      mixed: \"Mixed\",\n   141→    };\n   142→    return labels[f] || f;\n   143→  };\n   144→\n   145→  return (\n   146→    <div>\n   147→      <div className=\"text-center mb-6 sm:mb-8 mt-2 sm:mt-4\">\n   148→        <h1 className=\"text-2xl sm:text-4xl mb-2 sm:mb-3\" style={{ color: \"var(--color-text)\" }}>\n   149→          Find your reading companion\n   150→        </h1>\n   151→        <p\n   152→          className=\"text-sm sm:text-lg max-w-xl mx-auto\"\n   153→          style={{\n   154→            color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n   155→            fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\",\n   156→          }}\n   157→        >\n   158→          Browse open reading groups, join one that matches your pace, and\n   159→          connect on Telegram client\";\n\nimport { useEffect, useState, useCallback, useRef } from \"react\";\nimport Link from \"next/link\";\nimport Image from \"next/image\";\n\ninterface Listing {\n  id: number;\n  book_title: string;\n  book_author: string;\n  book_cover_url: string;\n  language: string;\n  reading_pace: string;\n  start_date: string;\n  meeting_format: string;\n  max_group_size: number;\n  requires_approval: number;\n  platform_preference: string;\n  member_count: number;\n  author_name: string;\n  created_at: string;\n}\n\nexport default function HomePage() {\n  const [listings, setListings] = useState<Listing[]>([]);\n  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);\n  const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null);\n\n  // Search & filter state\n  const [searchQuery, setSearchQuery] = useState(\"\");\n  const [meetingFormat, setMeetingFormat] = useState(\"\");\n  const [readingPace, setReadingPace] = useState(\"\");\n  const [startDateFrom, setStartDateFrom] = useState(\"\");\n  const [sort, setSort] = useState(\"newest\");\n  const [filtersOpen, setFiltersOpen] = useState(false);\n\n  // Pagination state\n  const [page, setPage] = useState(1);\n  const [totalPages, setTotalPages] = useState(1);\n  const [totalResults, setTotalResults] = useState(0);\n\n  const debounceRef = useRef<ReturnType<typeof setTimeout>>(undefined);\n\n  const fetchListings = useCallback(\n    (query: string, format: string, sortBy: string, pace?: string, dateFrom?: string, pageNum?: number) => {\n      setLoading(true);\n      const params = new URLSearchParams();\n      if (query) params.set(\"q\", query);\n      if (format) params.set(\"meeting_format\", format);\n      if (pace) params.set(\"reading_pace\", pace);\n      if (dateFrom) params.set(\"start_date_from\", dateFrom);\n      if (sortBy) params.set(\"sort\", sortBy);\n      if (pageNum && pageNum > 1) params.set(\"page\", String(pageNum));\n\n      const qs = params.toString();\n      fetch(`/api/listings${qs ? `?${qs}` : \"\"}`)\n        .then((r) => {\n          if (!r.ok) throw new Error(\"Failed to load listings\");\n          return r.json();\n        })\n        .then((data) => {\n          setListings(data.listings || []);\n          if (data.pagination) {\n            setTotalPages(data.pagination.totalPages || 1);\n            setTotalResults(data.pagination.total || 0);\n          }\n          setError(null);\n          setLoading(false);\n        })\n        .catch((err) => {\n          setError(err.message || \"Could not load listings. Please try again.\");\n          setLoading(false);\n        });\n    },\n    []\n  );\n\n  // Initial fetch\n  useEffect(() => {\n    fetchListings(\"\", \"\", \"newest\", \"\", \"\", 1);\n  }, [fetchListings]);\n\n  // Debounced search\n  const handleSearchChange = (value: string) => {\n    setSearchQuery(value);\n    setPage(1);\n    if (debounceRef.current) clearTimeout(debounceRef.current);\n    debounceRef.current = setTimeout(() => {\n      fetchListings(value, meetingFormat, sort, readingPace, startDateFrom, 1);\n    }, 400);\n  };\n\n  // Immediate filter changes\n  const handleFormatChange = (value: string) => {\n    setMeetingFormat(value);\n    setPage(1);\n    fetchListings(searchQuery, value, sort, readingPace, startDateFrom, 1);\n  };\n\n  const handlePaceChange = (value: string) => {\n    setReadingPace(value);\n    setPage(1);\n    fetchListings(searchQuery, meetingFormat, sort, value, startDateFrom, 1);\n  };\n\n  const handleStartDateFromChange = (value: string) => {\n    setStartDateFrom(value);\n    setPage(1);\n    fetchListings(searchQuery, meetingFormat, sort, readingPace, value, 1);\n  };\n\n  const handleSortChange = (value: string) => {\n    setSort(value);\n    setPage(1);\n    fetchListings(searchQuery, meetingFormat, value, readingPace, startDateFrom, 1);\n  };\n\n  const handlePageChange = (newPage: number) => {\n    setPage(newPage);\n    fetchListings(searchQuery, meetingFormat, sort, readingPace, startDateFrom, newPage);\n    window.scrollTo({ top: 0, behavior: \"smooth\" });\n  };\n\n  const clearFilters = () => {\n    setSearchQuery(\"\");\n    setMeetingFormat(\"\");\n    setReadingPace(\"\");\n    setStartDateFrom(\"\");\n    setSort(\"newest\");\n    setPage(1);\n    fetchListings(\"\", \"\", \"newest\", \"\", \"\", 1);\n  };\n\n  const hasActiveFilters = searchQuery || meetingFormat || readingPace || startDateFrom || sort !== \"newest\";\n\n  const formatMeetingType = (f: string) => {\n    const labels: Record<string, string> = {\n      voice: \"Voice calls\",\n      text: \"Text chat\",\n      mixed: \"Mixed\",\n    };\n    return labels[f] || f;\n  };\n\n  return (\n    <div>\n      <div className=\"text-center mb-6 sm:mb-8 mt-2 sm:mt-4\">\n        <h1 className=\"text-2xl sm:text-4xl mb-2 sm:mb-3\" style={{ color: \"var(--color-text)\" }}>\n          Find your reading companion\n        </h1>\n        <p\n          className=\"text-sm sm:text-lg max-w-xl mx-auto\"\n          style={{\n            color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n            fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\",\n          }}\n        >\n          Browse open reading groups, join one that matches your pace, and\n          connect on Telegram to read together.\n        </p>\n      </div>\n\n      {/* Search & Filter Bar */}\n      <div className=\"mb-6\">\n        <div className=\"flex gap-3 items-center\">\n          <div className=\"flex-1 relative\">\n            <input\n              type=\"text\"\n              placeholder=\"Search by book title or author...\"\n              value={searchQuery}\n              onChange={(e) => handleSearchChange(e.target.value)}\n              className=\"input-field\"\n              style={{ paddingLeft: \"2.5rem\" }}\n            />\n            <svg\n              aria-hidden=\"true\"\n              style={{\n                position: \"absolute\",\n                left: \"0.75rem\",\n                top: \"50%\",\n                transform: \"translateY(-50%)\",\n                color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n                width: \"1.125rem\",\n                height: \"1.125rem\",\n                pointerEvents: \"none\",\n              }}\n              fill=\"none\"\n              stroke=\"currentColor\"\n              strokeWidth={2}\n              viewBox=\"0 0 24 24\"\n            >\n              <circle cx={11} cy={11} r={8} />\n              <line x1={21} y1={21} x2={16.65} y2={16.65} />\n            </svg>\n          </div>\n          <button\n            onClick={() => setFiltersOpen(!filtersOpen)}\n            className=\"btn-secondary\"\n            aria-expanded={filtersOpen}\n            aria-controls=\"filter-panel\"\n            style={{\n              display: \"flex\",\n              alignItems: \"center\",\n              gap: \"0.375rem\",\n              whiteSpace: \"nowrap\",\n              position: \"relative\",\n            }}\n          >\n            <svg\n              aria-hidden=\"true\"\n              width=\"16\"\n              height=\"16\"\n              viewBox=\"0 0 24 24\"\n              fill=\"none\"\n              stroke=\"currentColor\"\n              strokeWidth={2}\n            >\n              <line x1={4} y1={6} x2={20} y2={6} />\n              <line x1={7} y1={12} x2={17} y2={12} />\n              <line x1={10} y1={18} x2={14} y2={18} />\n            </svg>\n            Filters\n            {hasActiveFilters && (\n              <span\n                aria-hidden=\"true\"\n                style={{\n                  width: \"0.5rem\",\n                  height: \"0.5rem\",\n                  borderRadius: \"50%\",\n                  backgroundColor: \"var(--color-accent)\",\n                  position: \"absolute\",\n                  top: \"0.375rem\",\n                  right: \"0.375rem\",\n                }}\n              />\n            )}\n          </button>\n        </div>\n\n        {/* Filter panel */}\n        {filtersOpen && (\n          <div\n            id=\"filter-panel\"\n            className=\"card mt-3\"\n            style={{ padding: \"1rem 1.25rem\" }}\n          >\n            <div\n              className=\"grid grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-4 gap-3 sm:gap-4 items-end\"\n              style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n            >\n              <div>\n                <label\n                  className=\"text-xs font-semibold mb-1 block\"\n                  style={{\n                    color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n                    textTransform: \"uppercase\",\n                    letterSpacing: \"0.05em\",\n                  }}\n                >\n                  Meeting format\n                </label>\n                <select\n                  value={meetingFormat}\n                  onChange={(e) => handleFormatChange(e.target.value)}\n                  className=\"input-field\"\n                  style={{ fontSize: \"0.875rem\", padding: \"0.5rem 0.75rem\" }}\n                >\n                  <option value=\"\">All formats</option>\n                  <option value=\"voice\">Voice calls</option>\n                  <option value=\"text\">Text chat</option>\n                  <option value=\"mixed\">Mixed</option>\n                </select>\n              </div>\n\n              <div>\n                <label\n                  className=\"text-xs font-semibold mb-1 block\"\n                  style={{\n                    color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n                    textTransform: \"uppercase\",\n                    letterSpacing: \"0.05em\",\n                  }}\n                >\n                  Reading pace\n                </label>\n                <select\n                  value={readingPace}\n                  onChange={(e) => handlePaceChange(e.target.value)}\n                  className=\"input-field\"\n                  style={{ fontSize: \"0.875rem\", padding: \"0.5rem 0.75rem\" }}\n                >\n                  <option value=\"\">All paces</option>\n                  <option value=\"chapter\">Chapter-based</option>\n                  <option value=\"pages\">Pages-based</option>\n                  <option value=\"week\">Weekly</option>\n                  <option value=\"day\">Daily</option>\n                </select>\n              </div>\n\n              <div>\n                <label\n                  className=\"text-xs font-semibold mb-1 block\"\n                  style={{\n                    color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n                    textTransform: \"uppercase\",\n                    letterSpacing: \"0.05em\",\n                  }}\n                >\n                  Starting from\n                </label>\n                <input\n                  type=\"date\"\n                  value={startDateFrom}\n                  onChange={(e) => handleStartDateFromChange(e.target.value)}\n                  className=\"input-field\"\n                  style={{ fontSize: \"0.875rem\", padding: \"0.5rem 0.75rem\" }}\n                />\n              </div>\n\n              <div>\n                <label\n                  className=\"text-xs font-semibold mb-1 block\"\n                  style={{\n                    color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n                    textTransform: \"uppercase\",\n                    letterSpacing: \"0.05em\",\n                  }}\n                >\n                  Sort by\n                </label>\n                <select\n                  value={sort}\n                  onChange={(e) => handleSortChange(e.target.value)}\n                  className=\"input-field\"\n                  style={{ fontSize: \"0.875rem\", padding: \"0.5rem 0.75rem\" }}\n                >\n                  <option value=\"newest\">Newest first</option>\n                  <option value=\"oldest\">Oldest first</option>\n                  <option value=\"start_date\">Start date (soonest)</option>\n                </select>\n              </div>\n\n              {hasActiveFilters && (\n                <button\n                  onClick={clearFilters}\n                  style={{\n                    color: \"var(--color-accent)\",\n                    fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\",\n                    fontSize: \"0.875rem\",\n                    fontWeight: 600,\n                    cursor: \"pointer\",\n                    background: \"none\",\n                    border: \"none\",\n                    padding: \"0.5rem 0\",\n                  }}\n                >\n                  Clear all\n                </button>\n              )}\n            </div>\n          </div>\n        )}\n      </div>\n\n      {/* Results */}\n      {loading ? (\n        <div className=\"grid gap-4\">\n          {[1, 2, 3].map((i) => (\n            <div key={i} className=\"card flex gap-4\">\n              <div\n                className=\"skeleton\"\n                style={{ width: \"4rem\", height: \"6rem\", flexShrink: 0 }}\n              />\n              <div className=\"flex-1\">\n                <div\n                  className=\"skeleton skeleton-heading\"\n                  style={{ width: `${60 - i * 5}%` }}\n                />\n                <div\n                  className=\"skeleton skeleton-text\"\n                  style={{ width: `${45 - i * 5}%` }}\n                />\n                <div className=\"flex gap-2 mt-2\">\n                  <div\n                    className=\"skeleton\"\n                    style={{\n                      width: \"5rem\",\n                      height: \"1.5rem\",\n                      borderRadius: \"9999px\",\n                    }}\n                  />\n                  <div\n                    className=\"skeleton\"\n                    style={{\n                      width: \"4rem\",\n                      height: \"1.5rem\",\n                      borderRadius: \"9999px\",\n                    }}\n                  />\n                  <div\n                    className=\"skeleton\"\n                    style={{\n                      width: \"5.5rem\",\n                      height: \"1.5rem\",\n                      borderRadius: \"9999px\",\n                    }}\n                  />\n                </div>\n              </div>\n            </div>\n          ))}\n        </div>\n      ) : error ? (\n        <div className=\"text-center py-16\">\n          <svg\n            width=\"40\"\n            height=\"40\"\n            viewBox=\"0 0 24 24\"\n            fill=\"none\"\n            stroke=\"var(--color-error)\"\n            strokeWidth={1.5}\n            style={{ margin: \"0 auto 0.75rem\" }}\n          >\n            <circle cx={12} cy={12} r={10} />\n            <line x1={12} y1={8} x2={12} y2={12} />\n            <line x1={12} y1={16} x2={12.01} y2={16} />\n          </svg>\n          <p\n            className=\"text-lg mb-2\"\n            style={{ color: \"var(--color-text)\", fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n          >\n            {error}\n          </p>\n          <button\n            onClick={() => fetchListings(searchQuery, meetingFormat, sort, readingPace, startDateFrom)}\n            className=\"btn-primary\"\n            style={{ marginTop: \"0.75rem\" }}\n          >\n            Try again\n          </button>\n        </div>\n      ) : listings.length === 0 ? (\n        <div className=\"text-center py-16\">\n          {hasActiveFilters ? (\n            <>\n              <p\n                className=\"text-lg mb-2\"\n                style={{ color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\" }}\n              >\n                No matching reading groups\n              </p>\n              <p\n                className=\"mb-6\"\n                style={{\n                  color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n                  fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\",\n                  fontSize: \"0.95rem\",\n                }}\n              >\n                Try adjusting your search or filters to find more groups.\n              </p>\n              <button onClick={clearFilters} className=\"btn-secondary\">\n                Clear filters\n              </button>\n            </>\n          ) : (\n            <>\n              <p\n                className=\"text-lg mb-2\"\n                style={{ color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\" }}\n              >\n                No reading groups yet\n              </p>\n              <p\n                className=\"mb-6\"\n                style={{\n                  color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n                  fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\",\n                  fontSize: \"0.95rem\",\n                }}\n              >\n                Be the first to create a reading group and find your book\n                companions.\n              </p>\n              <Link href=\"/listings/create\" className=\"btn-primary\">\n                Create the first listing\n              </Link>\n            </>\n          )}\n        </div>\n      ) : (\n        <>\n          <p\n            className=\"mb-3 text-sm\"\n            style={{\n              color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n              fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\",\n            }}\n          >\n            {totalResults} open group{totalResults !== 1 ? \"s\" : \"\"}\n            {hasActiveFilters ? \" found\" : \"\"}\n            {totalPages > 1 ? ` · Page ${page} of ${totalPages}` : \"\"}\n          </p>\n          <div className=\"grid gap-4\" role=\"list\" aria-label=\"Reading groups\">\n            {listings.map((listing) => (\n              <Link\n                key={listing.id}\n                href={`/listings/${listing.id}`}\n                className=\"card hover:shadow-md transition-shadow flex gap-4\"\n                role=\"listitem\"\n              >\n                {listing.book_cover_url ? (\n                  <Image\n                    src={listing.book_cover_url}\n                    alt={listing.book_title}\n                    width={64}\n                    height={96}\n                    className=\"w-16 h-24 object-cover rounded\"\n                    style={{ flexShrink: 0 }}\n                  />\n                ) : (\n                  <div\n                    className=\"w-16 h-24 rounded flex items-center justify-center text-2xl\"\n                    style={{\n                      backgroundColor: \"var(--color-border)\",\n                      flexShrink: 0,\n                      color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n                    }}\n                    aria-hidden=\"true\"\n                  >\n                    📖\n                  </div>\n                )}\n\n                <div className=\"flex-1 min-w-0\">\n                  <h3 className=\"text-lg font-bold truncate\">\n                    {listing.book_title}\n                  </h3>\n                  <p\n                    className=\"text-sm mb-2 truncate\"\n                    style={{\n                      color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n                      fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\",\n                    }}\n                  >\n                    by {listing.book_author} · posted by {listing.author_name}\n                  </p>\n\n                  <div\n                    className=\"flex flex-wrap gap-2\"\n                    style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n                  >\n                    <span\n                      className=\"badge\"\n                      style={{\n                        backgroundColor: \"rgba(224, 122, 58, 0.1)\",\n                        color: \"var(--color-accent)\",\n                      }}\n                    >\n                      {listing.reading_pace}\n                    </span>\n                    <span\n                      className=\"badge\"\n                      style={{\n                        backgroundColor: \"rgba(45, 138, 86, 0.1)\",\n                        color: \"var(--color-success)\",\n                      }}\n                    >\n                      {formatMeetingType(listing.meeting_format)}\n                    </span>\n                    <span\n                      className=\"badge\"\n                      style={{\n                        backgroundColor: \"rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05)\",\n                        color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n                      }}\n                    >\n                      Starts{\" \"}\n                      {new Date(listing.start_date).toLocaleDateString(\n                        \"en-US\",\n                        {\n                          month: \"short\",\n                          day: \"numeric\",\n                        }\n                      )}\n                    </span>\n                    <span\n                      className=\"badge\"\n                      style={{\n                        backgroundColor: \"rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05)\",\n                        color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n                      }}\n                    >\n                      {listing.member_count}/{listing.max_group_size} readers\n                    </span>\n                    {listing.requires_approval ? (\n                      <span\n                        className=\"badge\"\n                        style={{\n                          backgroundColor: \"rgba(224, 122, 58, 0.08)\",\n                          color: \"var(--color-accent)\",\n                        }}\n                      >\n                        Approval required\n                      </span>\n                    ) : null}\n                    {listing.platform_preference === \"discord\" ? (\n                      <span\n                        className=\"badge\"\n                        style={{\n                          backgroundColor: \"rgba(88, 101, 242, 0.08)\",\n                          color: \"#5865F2\",\n                          display: \"inline-flex\",\n                          alignItems: \"center\",\n                          gap: \"0.25rem\",\n                        }}\n                      >\n                        <svg width=\"10\" height=\"10\" viewBox=\"0 0 24 24\" fill=\"currentColor\">\n                          <path d=\"M20.317 4.3698a19.7913 19.7913             </div>\n                </div>\n              </Link>\n            ))}\n          </div>\n\n          {/* Pagination controls */}\n          {totalPages > 1 && (\n            <nav\n              className=\"flex justify-center items-center gap-2 mt-8\"\n              aria-label=\"Pagination\"\n              style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n            >\n              <button\n                onClick={() => handlePageChange(page - 1)}\n                disabled={page <= 1}\n                className=\"btn-secondary\"\n                style={{\n                  fontSize: \"0.875rem\",\n                  padding: \"0.5rem 1rem\",\n                  opacity: page <= 1 ? 0.5 : 1,\n                  cursor: page <= 1 ? \"not-allowed\" : \"pointer\",\n                }}\n                aria-label=\"Previous page\"\n              >\n                Previous\n              </button>\n              <span\n                className=\"text-sm\"\n onClick={() => handlePageChange(page + 1)}\n                disabled={page >= totalPages}\n                className=\"btn-secondary\"\n                style={{\n                  fontSize: \"0.875rem\",\n                  padding: \"0.5rem 1rem\",\n                  opacity: page >= totalPages ? 0.5 : 1,\n                  cursor: page >= totalPages ? \"not-allowed\" : \"pointer\",\n                }}\n                aria-label=\"Next page\"\n              >\n                Next\n              </button>\n            </nav>\n          )}\n        </>\n      )}\n    </div>\n {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01B5p9j3ATuGW777NKrhAaco","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"Let me assess the situation:\n\n1. Production is healthy\n2. All approved issues are closed\n3. No open PRs\n4. PAT expires in ~4 days (#54 needs-human)\n5. Telegram token (#21 needs-human)\n\nAll PRD features are implemented. I should look for meaningful improvements to propose and implement. Let me think about what would add the most value:\n\n1. **Stale listing cleanup** - Auto-close listings with start dates that have passed\n2. **UI polish** - Dark mode, improved design\n3. **\"My listings\" page** - Users can see listings they've created/joined\n\n\n4. **Listing edit/delete** - Authors should be able to modify their listings\n5. **Improved homepage** - Landing page for non-logged-in users\n\nLooking at what would actually matter most to users, a dashboard where people can manage their own groups and listings seems really valuable, along with automatically handling stale listings that have already started.\n\nThe \"My Groups\" dashboard feels like the highest priority—it gives returning users a clear place to see what they've created and manage it all. {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01KHiwnYuYs75syVCj4Rrv8g","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→\"use client\";\n     2→\n     3→import { useEffect, useState, useCallback, useRef } from \"react\";\n     4→import Link from \"next/link\";\n     5→import Image from \"next/image\";\n     6→\n     7→interface Listing {\n     8→  id: number;\n     9→  book_title: string;\n    10→  book_author: string;\n    11→  book_cover_url: string;\n    12→  language: string;\n    13→  reading_pace: string;\n    14→  start_date: string;\n    15→  meeting_format: string;\n    16→  max_group_size: number;\n    17→  requires_approval: number;\n    18→  platform_preference: string;\n    19→  member_count: number;\n    20→  author_name: string;\n    21→  created_at: string;\n    22→}\n    23→\n    24→export default function HomePage() {\n    25→  const [listings, setListings] = useState<Listing[]>([]);\n    26→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);\n    27→  const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null);\n    28→\n    29→  // Search & filter state\n    30→  const [searchQuery, setSearchQuery] = useState(\"\");\n    31→  const [meetingFormat, setMeetingFormat] = useState(\"\");\n    32→  const [readingPace, setReadingPace] = useState(\"\");\n    33→  const [startDateFrom, setStartDateFrom] = useState(\"\");\n    34→  const [sort, setSort] = useState(\"newest\");\n    35→  const [filtersOpen, setFiltersOpen] = useState(false);\n    36→\n    37→  // Pagination state\n    38→  const [page, setPage] = useState(1);\n    39→  const [totalPages, setTotalPages] = useState(1);\n    40→  const [totalResults, setTotalResults] = useState(0);\n    41→\n    42→  const debounceRef = useRef<ReturnType<typeof setTimeout>>(undefined);\n    43→\n    44→  const fetchListings = useCallback(\n    45→    (query: string, format: string, sortBy: string, pace?: string, dateFrom?: string, pageNum?: number) => {\n    46→      setLoading(true);\n    47→      const params = new URLSearchParams();\n    48→      if (query) params.set(\"q\", query);\n    49→      if (format) params.set(\"meeting_format\", format);\n    50→      if (pace) params.set(\"reading_pace\", pace);\n    51→      if (dateFrom) params.set(\"start_date_from\", dateFrom);\n    52→      if (sortBy) params.set(\"sort\", sortBy);\n    53→      if (pageNum && pageNum > 1) params.set(\"page\", String(pageNum));\n    54→\n    55→      const qs = params.toString();\n    56→      fetch(`/api/listings${qs ? `?${qs}` : \"\"}`)\n    57→        .then((r) => {\n    58→          if (!r.ok) throw new Error(\"Failed to load listings\");\n    59→          return r.json();\n    60→        })\n    61→        .then((data) => {\n    62→          setListings(data.listings || []);\n    63→          if (data.pagination) {\n    64→            setTotalPages(data.pagination.totalPages || 1);\n    65→            setTotalResults(data.pagination.total || 0);\n    66→          }\n    67→          setError(null);\n    68→          setLoading(false);\n    69→        })\n    70→        .catch((err) => {\n    71→          setError(err.message || \"Could not load listings. Please try again.\");\n    72→          setLoading(false);\n    73→        });\n    74→    },\n    75→    []\n    76→  );\n    77→\n    78→  // Initial fetch\n    79→  useEffect(() => {\n    80→    fetchListings(\"\", \"\", \"newest\", \"\", \"\", 1);\n    81→  }, [fetchListings]);\n    82→\n    83→  // Debounced search\n    84→  const handleSearchChange = (value: string) => {\n    85→    setSearchQuery(value);\n    86→    setPage(1);\n    87→    if (debounceRef.current) clearTimeout(debounceRef.current);\n    88→    debounceRef.current = setTimeout(() => {\n    89→      fetchListings(value, meetingFormat, sort, readingPace, startDateFrom, 1);\n    90→    }, 400);\n    91→  };\n    92→\n    93→  // Immediate filter changes\n    94→  const handleFormatChange = (value: string) => {\n    95→    setMeetingFormat(value);\n    96→    setPage(1);\n    97→    fetchListings(searchQuery, value, sort, readingPace, startDateFrom, 1);\n    98→  };\n    99→\n   100→  const handlePaceChange = (value: string) => {\n   101→    setReadingPace(value);\n   102→    setPage(1);\n   103→    fetchListings(searchQuery, meetingFormat, sort, value, startDateFrom, 1);\n   104→  };\n   105→\n   106→  const handleStartDateFromChange = (value: string) => {\n   107→    setStartDateFrom(value);\n   108→    setPage(1);\n   109→    fetchListings(searchQuery, meetingFormat, sort, readingPace, value, 1);\n   110→  };\n   111→\n   112→  const handleSortChange = (value: string) => {\n   113→    setSort(value);\n   114→    setPage(1);\n   115→    fetchListings(searchQuery, meetingFormat, value, readingPace, startDateFrom, 1);\n   116→  };\n   117→\n   118→  const handlePageChange = (newPage: number) => {\n   119→    setPage(newPage);\n   120→    fetchListings(searchQuery, meetingFormat, sort, readingPace, startDateFrom, newPage);\n   121→    window.scrollTo({ top: 0, behavior: \"smooth\" });\n   122→  };\n   123→\n   124→  const clearFilters = () => {\n   125→    setSearchQuery(\"\");\n   126→    setMeetingFormat(\"\");\n   127→    setReadingPace(\"\");\n   128→    setStartDateFrom(\"\");\n   129→    setSort(\"newest\");\n   130→    setPage(1);\n   131→    fetchListings(\"\", \"\", \"newest\", \"\", \"\", 1);\n   132→  };\n   133→\n   134→  const hasActiveFilters = searchQuery || meetingFormat || readingPace || startDateFrom || sort !== \"newest\";\n   135→\n   136→  const formatMeetingType = (f: string) => {\n   137→    const labels: Record<string, string> = {\n   138→      voice: \"Voice calls\",\n   139→      text: \"Text chat\",\n   140→      mixed: \"Mixed\",\n   141→    };\n   142→    return labels[f] || f;\n   143→  };\n   144→\n   145→  return (\n   146→    <div>\n   147→      <div className=\"text-center mb-6 sm:mb-8 mt-2 sm:mt-4\">\n   148→        <h1 className=\"text-2xl sm:text-4xl mb-2 sm:mb-3\" style={{ color: \"var(--color-text)\" }}>\n   149→          Find your reading companion\n   150→        </h1>\n   151→        <p\n   152→          className=\"text-sm sm:text-lg max-w-xl mx-auto\"\n   153→          style={{\n   154→            color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n   155→            fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\",\n   156→          }}\n   157→        >\n   158→          Browse open reading groups, join one that matches your pace, and\n   159→          connect on Telegram to read together.\n   160→        </p>\n   161→      </div>\n   162→\n   163→      {/* Search & Filter Bar */}\n   164→      <div className=\"mb-6\">\n   165→        <div className=\"flex gap-3 items-center\">\n   166→          <div className=\"flex-1 relative\">\n   167→            <input\n   168→              type=\"text\"\n   169→              placeholder=\"Search by book title or author...\"\n   170→              value={searchQuery}\n   171→              onChange={(e) => handleSearchChange(e.target.value)}\n   172→              className=\"input-field\"\n   173→              style={{ paddingLeft: \"2.5rem\" }}\n   174→            />\n   175→            <svg\n   176→              aria-hidden=\"true\"\n   177→              style={{\n   178→                position:       <div>\n   252→                <label\n   253→                  className=\"text-xs font-semibold mb-1 block\"\n   254→                  style={{\n   255→                    color: \"next/link\";\n     4→import { useEffect, useState, useRef } from \"react\";\n     5→import { useRouter } from \"next/navigation\";\n     6→\n     7→interface User {\n     8→  id: number;\n     9→  email: string;\n    10→  displayName: string;\n    11→}\n    12→\n    13→export default function Navbar() {\n    14→  const [user, setUser] = useState<User | null>(null);\n    15→  const [notifCount, setNotifCount] = useState(0);\n    16→  const [menuOpen, setMenuOpen] = useState(false);\n    17→  const [mobileMenuOpen, setMobileMenuOpen] = useState(false);\n    18→  const router = useRouter();\n    19→  const menuRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);\n    20→\n    21→  useEffect(() => {\n    22→    fetch(\"/api/auth/me\")\n    23→      .then((r) => r.json())\n    24→      .then((data) => {\n    25→        if (data.user) {\n    26→          setUser(data.user);\n    27→          setNotifCount(data.user.unreadNotificationCount || 0);\n    28→        }\n    29→      })\n    30→      .catch(() => {\n    31→        // Auth check failed — stay logged out\n    32→      });\n    33→  }, []);\n    34→\n    35→  // Close menus on outside click\n    36→  useEffect(() => {\n    37→    const handleClickOutside = (e: MouseEvent) => {\n    38→      if (menuRef.current && !menuRef.current.contains(e.target as Node)) {\n    39→        setMenuOpen(false);\n    40→      }\n    41→    };\n    42→    document.addEventListener(\"mousedown\", handleClickOutside);\n    43→    return () => document.removeEventListener(\"mousedown\", handleClickOutside);\n    44→  }, []);\n    45→\n    46→  // Close mobile menu on route change\n    47→  useEffect(() => {\n    48→    setMobileMenuOpen(false);\n    49→    setMenuOpen(false);\n    50→  }, []);\n    51→\n    52→  const handleLogout = async () => {\n    53→    await fetch(\"/api/auth/logout\", { method: \"POST\" });\n    54→    setUser(null);\n    55→    setMenuOpen(false);\n    56→    setMobileMenuOpen(false);\n    57→    router.push(\"/\");\n    58→    router.refresh();\n    59→  };\n    60→\n    61→  return (\n    62→    <nav\n    63→      className=\"border-b sticky top-0 z-50\"\n    64→      style={{\n    65→        borderColor: \"var(--color-border)\",\n    66→        backgroundColor: \"var(--color-bg)\",\n    67→      }}\n    68→    >\n    69→      <div className=\"max-w-5xl mx-auto px-3 sm:px-4 h-14 sm:h-16 flex items-center justify-between\">\n    70→        <Link\n    71→          href=\"/\"\n    72→          className=\"text-lg sm:text-xl font-bold\"\n    73→          style={{ color: \"var(--color-accent)\", letterSpacing: \"-0.03em\" }}\n    74→          onClick={() => setMobileMenuOpen(false)}\n    75→        >\n    76→          bookmate\n    77→        </Link>\n    78→\n    79→        {/* Desktop nav */}\n    80→        <div className=\"hidden sm:flex items-center gap-4\">\n    81→          {user ? (\n    82→            <>\n    83→              <Link\n    84→                href=\"/listings/create\"\n    85→                className=\"btn-primary text-sm\"\n    86→                style={{ padding: \"0.5rem 1rem\" }}\n    87→              >\n    88→                + New Listing\n    89→              </Link>\n    90→\n    91→              <div className=\"relative\" ref={menuRef}>\n    92→                <button\n    93→                  onClick={() => setMenuOpen(!menuOpen)}\n    94→                  className=\"flex items-center gap-2 cursor-pointer\"\n    95→                  style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif\" }}\n    96→                  aria-label={`User menu for ${user.displayName}${notifCount > 0 ? `, ${notifCount} unread notifications` : className=\"w-8 h-8 rounded-full flex items-center justify-center text-sm font-bold text-white\"\n   102→                    style={{ backgroundColor: \"var(--color-accent)\" }}\n   103→
