{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_0147Q8N73pjSPrKmVZ4ZwKum","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import db from \"./db\";\n     2→import { sendEmail } from \"./email\";\n     3→\n     4→function getUserEmail(userId: number): string | null {\n     5→  const user = db\n     6→    .prepare(\"SELECT email FROM users WHERE id = ?\")\n     7→    .get(userId) as { email: string } | undefined;\n     8→  return user?.email || null;\n     9→}\n    10→\n    11→export function createNotification(\n    12→  userId: number,\n    13→  listingId: number,\n    14→  type: string,\n    15→  message: string\n    16→) {\n    17→  db.prepare(\n    18→    \"INSERT INTO notifications (user_id, listing_id, type, message) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n    19→  ).run(userId, listingId, type, message);\n    20→\n    21→  // Also send email notification (fire-and-forget)\n    22→  const email = getUserEmail(userId);\n    23→  if (email) {\n    24→    const subject = getEmailSubject(type);\n    25→    sendEmail(email, subject, message).catch(() => {\n    26→      // Email sending is best-effort — don't block on failure\n    27→    });\n    28→  }\n    29→}\n    30→\n    31→function getEmailSubject(type: string): string {\n    32→  switch (type) {\n    33→    case \"new_member\":\n    34→      return \"Bookmate: Someone joined your reading group!\";\n    35→    case \"group_full\":\n    36→      return \"Bookmate: Your reading group is full!\";\n    37→    case \"application_received\":\n    38→      return \"Bookmate: New application for your reading group\";\n    39→    case \"application_approved\":\n    40→      return \"Bookmate: Your application was approved!\";\n    41→    case \"application_rejected\":\n    42→      return \"Bookmate: Application update\";\n    43→    case \"rating_received\":\n    44→      return \"Bookmate: You received a new rating\";\n    45→    default:\n    46→      return \"Bookmate: Notification\";\n    47→  }\n    48→}\n    49→\n    50→export function notifyListingAuthor(\n    51→  listingId: number,\n    52→  joinerName: string\n    53→) {\n    54→  const listing = db\n    55→    .prepare(\n    56→      \"SELECT l.author_id, l.book_title FROM listings l WHERE l.id = ?\"\n    57→    )\n    58→    .get(listingId) as { author_id: number; book_title: string } | undefined;\n    59→\n    60→  if (listing) {\n    61→    createNotification(\n    62→      listing.author_id,\n    63→      listingId,\n    64→      \"new_member\",\n    65→      `${joinerName} joined your reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\"`\n    66→    );\n    67→  }\n    68→}\n    69→\n    70→export function notifyApplicationReceived(\n    71→  listingId: number,\n    72→  applicantName: string\n    73→) {\n    74→  const listing = db\n    75→    .prepare(\n    76→      \"SELECT l.author_id, l.book_title FROM listings l WHERE l.id = ?\"\n    77→    )\n    78→    .get(listingId) as { author_id: number; book_title: string } | undefined;\n    79→\n    80→  if (listing) {\n    81→    createNotification(\n    82→      listing.author_id,\n    83→      listingId,\n    84→      \"application_received\",\n    85→      `${applicantName} applied to join your reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\"`\n    86→    );\n    87→  }\n    88→}\n    89→\n    90→export function notifyApplicationDecision(\n    91→  userId: number,\n    92→  listingId: number,\n    93→  decision: \"approved\" | \"rejected\"\n    94→) {\n    95→  const listing = db\n    96→    .prepare(\"SELECT book_title FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    97→    .get(listingId) as { book_title: string } | undefined;\n    98→\n    99→  if (listing) {\n   100→    const type = decision === \"approved\" ? \"application_approved\" : \"application_rejected\";\n   101→    const message =\n   102→      decision === \"approved\"\n   103→        ? `Your application to join \"${listing.book_title}\" was approved!`\n   104→        : `Your application to join \"${listing.book_title}\" was not accepted.`;\n   105→    createNotification(userId, listingId, type, message);\n   106→  }\n   107→}\n   108→\n   109→export function notifyGroupFull(listingId: number) {\n   110→  const listing = db\n   111→    .prepare(\"SELECT book_title FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n   112→    .get(listingId) as { book_title: string } | undefined;\n   113→  const members = db\n   114→    .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n   115→    .all(listingId) as { user_id: number }[];\n   116→\n   117→  if (listing) {\n   118→    const botConfigured = !!process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN;\n   119→    const message = botConfigured\n   120→      ? `The reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" is now full! The Telegram group will be set up automatically.`\n   121→      : `The reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" is now full! The organizer will share a Telegram link soon.`;\n   122→\n   123→    for (const member of members) {\n   124→      createNotification(\n   125→        member.user_id,\n   126→        listingId,\n   127→        \"group_full\",\n   128→        message\n   129→      );\n   130→    }\n   131→  }\n   132→}\n   133→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01GwLJUt1Yq3iqG4wELfymEH","session_id":"e8f30eef-be0b-49af-a8d3-712787ead3bb","uuid":"50260a04-2025-4d69-a436-ae79a4d1f845","timestamp":"2026-03-23T11:28:04.232Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_012bZoewResCNMiM6hVDdEdP","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { checkRateLimit } from \"@/lib/rate-limit\";\n     5→\n     6→export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n     7→  const session = await requireAuth();\n     8→  if (!session) {\n     9→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    10→  }\n    11→\n    12→  // Rate limit: 10 listings per 15 minutes per user\n    13→  const rateLimitKey = `create-listing:${session.userId}`;\n    14→  const { allowed, retryAfter } = checkRateLimit(rateLimitKey, 10);\n    15→  if (!allowed) {\n    16→    return NextResponse.json(\n    17→      { error: \"Too many listings created. Please try again later.\" },\n    18→      { status: 429, headers: { \"Retry-After\": String(retryAfter) } }\n    19→    );\n    20→  }\n    21→\n    22→  try {\n    23→    const body = await req.json();\n    24→    const {\n    25→      bookTitle,\n    26→      bookAuthor,\n    27→      bookCoverUrl,\n    28→      bookOlid,\n    29→      language,\n    30→      readingPace,\n    31→      startDate,\n    32→      meetingFormat,\n    33→      maxGroupSize,\n    34→      requiresApproval,\n    35→      platformPreference,\n    36→    } = body;\n    37→\n    38→    if (!bookTitle || !bookAuthor || !readingPace || !startDate || !meetingFormat || !maxGroupSize) {\n    39→      return NextResponse.json(\n    40→        { error: \"Missing required fields\" },\n    41→        { status: 400 }\n    42→      );\n    43→    }\n    44→\n    45→    // Input length limits to prevent abuse\n    46→    if (bookTitle.length > 300 || bookAuthor.length > 200 || readingPace.length > 200) {\n    47→      return NextResponse.json(\n    48→        { error: \"One or more fields exceed maximum length\" },\n    49→        { status: 400 }\n    50→      );\n    51→    }\n    52→\n    53→    if (![\"voice\", \"text\", \"mixed\"].includes(meetingFormat)) {\n    54→      return NextResponse.json(\n    55→        { error: \"Invalid meeting format\" },\n    56→        { status: 400 }\n    57→      );\n    58→    }\n    59→\n    60→    if (!startDate || !/^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}$/.test(startDate)) {\n    61→      return NextResponse.json(\n    62→        { error: \"Invalid start date format (expected YYYY-MM-DD)\" },\n    63→        { status: 400 }\n    64→      );\n    65→    }\n    66→\n    67→    const size = parseInt(maxGroupSize, 10);\n    68→    if (isNaN(size) || size < 2 || size > 20) {\n    69→      return NextResponse.json(\n    70→        { error: \"Group size must be between 2 and 20\" },\n    71→        { status: 400 }\n    72→      );\n    73→    }\n    74→\n    75→    const platform = [\"telegram\", \"discord\"].includes(platformPreference)\n    76→      ? platformPreference\n    77→      : \"telegram\";\n    78→\n    79→    // Validate book cover URL — only allow Open Library covers to prevent SSRF/tracking\n    80→    const sanitizedCoverUrl =\n    81→      bookCoverUrl && typeof bookCoverUrl === \"string\" &&\n    82→      bookCoverUrl.startsWith(\"https://covers.openlibrary.org/\")\n    83→        ? bookCoverUrl\n    84→        : \"\";\n    85→\n    86→    // Wrap listing creation + auto-join in a transaction for atomicity\n    87→    const createTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n    88→      const result = db\n    89→        .prepare(\n    90→          `INSERT INTO listings (author_id, book_title, book_author, book_cover_url, book_olid, language, reading_pace, start_date, meeting_format, max_group_size, requires_approval, platform_preference)\n    91→           VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`\n    92→        )\n    93→        .run(\n    94→          session.userId,\n    95→          bookTitle.trim(),\n    96→          bookAuthor.trim(),\n    97→          sanitizedCoverUrl,\n    98→          bookOlid || \"\",\n    99→          language || \"English\",\n   100→          readingPace.trim(),\n   101→          startDate,\n   102→          meetingFormat,\n   103→          size,\n   104→          requiresApproval ? 1 : 0,\n   105→          platform\n   106→        );\n   107→\n   108→      // Auto-join the author as a member\n   109→      db.prepare(\n   110→        \"INSERT INTO listing_members (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n   111→      ).run(result.lastInsertRowid, session.userId);\n   112→\n   113→      return result.lastInsertRowid;\n   114→    });\n   115→\n   116→    const listingId = createTransaction();\n   117→\n   118→    return NextResponse.json(\n   119→      { id: listingId },\n   120→      { status: 201 }\n   121→    );\n   122→  } catch (error) {\n   123→    console.error(\"Create listing error:\", error);\n   124→    return NextResponse.json(\n   125→      { error: \"Internal server error\" },\n   126→      { status: 500 }\n   127→    );\n   128→  }\n   129→}\n   130→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01GwLJUt1Yq3iqG4wELfymEH","session_id":"e8f30eef-be0b-49af-a8d3-712787ead3bb","uuid":"16f092b6-13cc-4eb6-bb25-3f5532b26327","timestamp":"2026-03-23T11:28:11.831Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01CgTagLxDgpcaEQSHs9xKVM","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { notifyListingAuthor, notifyGroupFull } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n     5→\n     6→export async function POST(\n     7→  _req: NextRequest,\n     8→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n     9→) {\n    10→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    11→  if (!session) {\n    12→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    13→  }\n    14→\n    15→  const { id } = await params;\n    16→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    17→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    18→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n    19→  }\n    20→\n    21→  const listing = db\n    22→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    23→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    24→\n    25→  if (!listing) {\n    26→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    27→  }\n    28→\n    29→  // Author is already a member — don't allow re-joining\n    30→  if (listing.author_id === session.userId) {\n    31→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"You are the organizer of this group\" }, { status: 400 });\n    32→  }\n    33→\n    34→  if (listing.requires_approval) {\n    35→    return NextResponse.json(\n    36→      { error: \"This group requires approval. Please apply instead.\" },\n    37→      { status: 400 }\n    38→    );\n    39→  }\n    40→\n    41→  if (listing.is_full) {\n    42→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"This group is already full\" }, { status: 400 });\n    43→  }\n    44→\n    45→  // Check if already a member\n    46→  const existing = db\n    47→    .prepare(\n    48→      \"SELECT 1 FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ?\"\n    49→    )\n    50→    .get(listingId, session.userId);\n    51→\n    52→  if (existing) {\n    53→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"You are already a member\" }, { status: 400 });\n    54→  }\n    55→\n    56→  // Wrap count check + insert in a transaction to prevent race conditions\n    57→  const joinTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n    58→    const { count } = db\n    59→      .prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n    60→      .get(listingId) as { count: number };\n    61→\n    62→    if (count >= (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n    63→      return { error: \"This group is already full\", count };\n    64→    }\n    65→\n    66→    db.prepare(\n    67→      \"INSERT INTO listing_members (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n    68→    ).run(listingId, session.userId);\n    69→\n    70→    const newCount = count + 1;\n    71→    if (newCount >= (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n    72→      db.prepare(\"UPDATE listings SET is_full = 1 WHERE id = ?\").run(listingId);\n    73→    }\n    74→\n    75→    return { error: null, count: newCount };\n    76→  });\n    77→\n    78→  const result = joinTransaction();\n    79→\n    80→  if (result.error) {\n    81→    return NextResponse.json({ error: result.error }, { status: 400 });\n    82→  }\n    83→\n    84→  // Notify outside the transaction (fire-and-forget)\n    85→  notifyListingAuthor(listingId, session.displayName || \"Someone\");\n    86→\n    87→  if (result.count === (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n    88→    notifyGroupFull(listingId);\n    89→  }\n    90→\n    91→  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true, memberCount: result.count });\n    92→}\n    93→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01GwLJUt1Yq3iqG4wELfymEH","session_id":"e8f30eef-be0b-49af-a8d3-712787ead3bb","uuid":"e5de2496-0871-43ab-9591-9ddcf14f823a","timestamp":"2026-03-23T11:28:11.961Z"}

{"type":"system","subtype":"task_progress","task_id":"a3d5c06c8cdbedb7f","tool_use_id":"toolu_01GwLJUt1Yq3iqG4wELfymEH","description":"Reading app/app/api/listings/[id]/applications/route.ts","usage":{"total_tokens":45269,"tool_uses":17,"duration_ms":16374},"last_tool_name":"Read","uuid":"bcce9b6b-b5ff-4d27-b228-3bde9068b94c","session_id":"e8f30eef-be0b-49af-a8d3-712787ead3bb"}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_016u9VWsV483uiR2Wq3MmdJ3","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01U2mNX2B8A4EbHQKoxXmNMb","name":"Read","input":{"file_path":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/applications/route.ts"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":3026,"cache_read_input_tokens":42021,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":3026,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":33,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01GwLJUt1Yq3iqG4wELfymEH","session_id":"e8f30eef-be0b-49af-a8d3-712787ead3bb","uuid":"0d75e85c-0793-41f0-8dcd-a2f70d0deead"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01U2mNX2B8A4EbHQKoxXmNMb","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { notifyApplicationDecision, notifyGroupFull } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n     5→\n     6→export async function PATCH(\n     7→  req: NextRequest,\n     8→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n     9→) {\n    10→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    11→  if (!session) {\n    12→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    13→  }\n    14→\n    15→  const { id } = await params;\n    16→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    17→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    18→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n    19→  }\n    20→\n    21→  const listing = db\n    22→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    23→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    24→\n    25→  if (!listing) {\n    26→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    27→  }\n    28→\n    29→  if (listing.author_id !== session.userId) {\n    30→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Only the organizer can manage applications\" }, { status: 403 });\n    31→  }\n    32→\n    33→  const body = await req.json();\n    34→  const { applicationId, action } = body;\n    35→\n    36→  if (!applicationId || ![\"approve\", \"reject\"].includes(action)) {\n    37→    return NextResponse.json(\n    38→      { error: \"Invalid request. Provide applicationId and action (approve/reject).\" },\n    39→      { status: 400 }\n    40→    );\n    41→  }\n    42→\n    43→  // Verify application belongs to this listing and is pending\n    44→  const application = db\n    45→    .prepare(\n    46→      \"SELECT * FROM listing_applications WHERE id = ? AND listing_id = ? AND status = 'pending'\"\n    47→    )\n    48→    .get(applicationId, listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    49→\n    50→  if (!application) {\n    51→    return NextResponse.json(\n    52→      { error: \"Application not found or already decided\" },\n    53→      { status: 404 }\n    54→    );\n    55→  }\n    56→\n    57→  const applicantUserId = application.user_id as number;\n    58→\n    59→  if (action === \"approve\") {\n    60→    // Use transaction to ensure atomicity\n    61→    const approveTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n    62→      // Check if group is already full\n    63→      const { count } = db\n    64→        .prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n    65→        .get(listingId) as { count: number };\n    66→\n    67→      if (count >= (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n    68→        throw new Error(\"Group is already full\");\n    69→      }\n    70→\n    71→      // Update application status\n    72→      db.prepare(\n    73→        \"UPDATE listing_applications SET status = 'approved', decided_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?\"\n    74→      ).run(applicationId);\n    75→\n    76→      // Add to members\n    77→      db.prepare(\n    78→        \"INSERT INTO listing_members (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n    79→      ).run(listingId, applicantUserId);\n    80→\n    81→      // Check if group is now full\n    82→      const newCount = count + 1;\n    83→      let rejectedApps: { id: number; user_id: number }[] = [];\n    84→      if (newCount >= (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n    85→        db.prepare(\"UPDATE listings SET is_full = 1 WHERE id = ?\").run(listingId);\n    86→\n    87→        // Reject remaining pending applications inside the transaction\n    88→        rejectedApps = db\n    89→          .prepare(\n    90→            \"SELECT id, user_id FROM listing_applications WHERE listing_id = ? AND status = 'pending'\"\n    91→          )\n    92→          .all(listingId) as { id: number; user_id: number }[];\n    93→\n    94→        for (const app of rejectedApps) {\n    95→          db.prepare(\n    96→            \"UPDATE listing_applications SET status = 'rejected', decided_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?\"\n    97→          ).run(app.id);\n    98→        }\n    99→\n   100→        return { isFull: true, rejectedApps };\n   101→      }\n   102→      return { isFull: false, rejectedApps };\n   103→    });\n   104→\n   105→    try {\n   106→      const result = approveTransaction();\n   107→\n   108→      // Send notifications outside the transaction (non-critical)\n   109→      try {\n   110→        notifyApplicationDecision(applicantUserId, listingId, \"approved\");\n   111→\n   112→        if (result.isFull) {\n   113→          notifyGroupFull(listingId);\n   114→          for (const app of result.rejectedApps) {\n   115→            notifyApplicationDecision(app.user_id, listingId, \"rejected\");\n   116→          }\n   117→        }\n   118→      } catch (notifErr) {\n   119→        console.error(\"Notification error (approval succeeded):\", notifErr);\n   120→      }\n   121→\n   122→      return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n   123→    } catch (err) {\n   124→      const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : \"Failed to approve\";\n   125→      return NextResponse.json({ error: message }, { status: 400 });\n   126→    }\n   127→  } else {\n   128→    // Reject\n   129→    db.prepare(\n   130→      \"UPDATE listing_applications SET status = 'rejected', decided_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?\"\n   131→    ).run(applicationId);\n   132→\n   133→    notifyApplicationDecision(applicantUserId, listingId, \"rejected\");\n   134→\n   135→    return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n   136→  }\n   137→}\n   138→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01GwLJUt1Yq3iqG4wELfymEH","session_id":"e8f30eef-be0b-49af-a8d3-712787ead3bb","uuid":"70f8e0da-04fe-437e-a8a1-99585d2f298e","timestamp":"2026-03-23T11:28:14.804Z"}

{"type":"system","subtype":"task_progress","task_id":"a3d5c06c8cdbedb7f","tool_use_id":"toolu_01GwLJUt1Yq3iqG4wELfymEH","description":"Reading app/app/api/listings/[id]/apply/route.ts","usage":{"total_tokens":47212,"tool_uses":19,"duration_ms":20350},"last_tool_name":"Read","uuid":"54915dd2-811f-41c1-bef5-2c6eea21c482","session_id":"e8f30eef-be0b-49af-a8d3-712787ead3bb"}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01R34w1xmyt1YfK7y52Y1sfT","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_011UC5b8H4SFKAiqDap8Rw3v","name":"Read","input":{"file_path":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/apply/route.ts"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1931,"cache_read_input_tokens":45047,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":1931,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":4,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01GwLJUt1Yq3iqG4wELfymEH","session_id":"e8f30eef-be0b-49af-a8d3-712787ead3bb","uuid":"38fca4a9-b3c7-41ce-a64d-602387d2b139"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_011UC5b8H4SFKAiqDap8Rw3v","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { notifyApplicationReceived } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n     5→\n     6→export async function POST(\n     7→  _req: NextRequest,\n     8→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n     9→) {\n    10→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    11→  if (!session) {\n    12→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    13→  }\n    14→\n    15→  const { id } = await params;\n    16→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    17→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    18→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n    19→  }\n    20→\n    21→  const listing = db\n    22→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    23→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    24→\n    25→  if (!listing) {\n    26→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    27→  }\n    28→\n    29→  if (listing.author_id === session.userId) {\n    30→    return NextResponse.json(\n    31→      { error: \"You cannot apply to your own listing\" },\n    32→      { status: 400 }\n    33→    );\n    34→  }\n    35→\n    36→  if (!listing.requires_approval) {\n    37→    return NextResponse.json(\n    38→      { error: \"This group does not require approval. Join directly.\" },\n    39→      { status: 400 }\n    40→    );\n    41→  }\n    42→\n    43→  if (listing.is_full) {\n    44→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"This group is already full\" }, { status: 400 });\n    45→  }\n    46→\n    47→  // Wrap membership check + application in a transaction to prevent TOCTOU races\n    48→  const applyTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n    49→    // Check if already a member\n    50→    const isMember = db\n    51→      .prepare(\"SELECT 1 FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ?\")\n    52→      .get(listingId, session.userId);\n    53→\n    54→    if (isMember) {\n    55→      return { error: \"You are already a member\", status: 400 };\n    56→    }\n    57→\n    58→    // Re-check is_full inside transaction\n    59→    const currentListing = db\n    60→      .prepare(\"SELECT is_full FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    61→      .get(listingId) as { is_full: number } | undefined;\n    62→\n    63→    if (currentListing?.is_full) {\n    64→      return { error: \"This group is already full\", status: 400 };\n    65→    }\n    66→\n    67→    // Check if already applied (pending)\n    68→    const existingApp = db\n    69→      .prepare(\n    70→        \"SELECT id, status FROM listing_applications WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ?\"\n    71→      )\n    72→      .get(listingId, session.userId) as { id: number; status: string } | undefined;\n    73→\n    74→    if (existingApp) {\n    75→      if (existingApp.status === \"pending\") {\n    76→        return { error: \"You have already applied\", status: 400 };\n    77→      }\n    78→      if (existingApp.status === \"approved\") {\n    79→        return { error: \"You are already approved\", status: 400 };\n    80→      }\n    81→      // If rejected, allow re-application by updating status back to pending\n    82→      db.prepare(\n    83→        \"UPDATE listing_applications SET status = 'pending', decided_at = NULL, applied_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?\"\n    84→      ).run(existingApp.id);\n    85→\n    86→      return { error: null, status: 200 };\n    87→    }\n    88→\n    89→    // Insert new application\n    90→    db.prepare(\n    91→      \"INSERT INTO listing_applications (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n    92→    ).run(listingId, session.userId);\n    93→\n    94→    return { error: null, status: 200 };\n    95→  });\n    96→\n    97→  const result = applyTransaction();\n    98→\n    99→  if (result.error) {\n   100→    return NextResponse.json({ error: result.error }, { status: result.status });\n   101→  }\n   102→\n   103→  // Notify outside transaction (fire-and-forget)\n   104→  notifyApplicationReceived(listingId, session.displayName || \"Someone\");\n   105→\n   106→  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n   107→}\n   108→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01GwLJUt1Yq3iqG4wELfymEH","session_id":"e8f30eef-be0b-49af-a8d3-712787ead3bb","uuid":"0e730676-531d-409e-9ea7-e0138c82d749","timestamp":"2026-03-23T11:28:18.788Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01GwLJUt1Yq3iqG4wELfymEH","type":"tool_result","content":[{"type":"text","text":"I now have a thorough understanding of all the relevant code. Let me compile the findings.\n\n---\n\n## Code Review: Bookmate Next.js Application\n\nReviewing: `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/page.tsx`, `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/listings/[id]/page.tsx` + `ListingDetail.tsx`, `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/listings/create/page.tsx`, `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/profile/page.tsx`, `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/route.ts`, `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts`, `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/db.ts`, `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/session.ts`, `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/notifications.ts`\n\n---\n\n## Critical Issues\n\n### 1. Race condition: stale `is_full` flag used before transaction in `/join` and `/apply` routes\n\n**Confidence: 92**\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts`, lines 41-43\n\n```ts\nif (listing.is_full) {\n  return NextResponse.json({ error: \"This group is already full\" }, { status: 400 });\n}\n```\n\nThe `is_full` check at line 41 happens *before* the transaction at line 57. The `listing` object was fetched at line 21. Between that fetch and the transaction, another request may have filled the group and set `is_full = 1` — but the pre-transaction check passes because it reads a stale value. The transaction does re-count members and correctly gates the insert, so the actual double-join is prevented. However, the pre-transaction `is_full` guard is still misleading: it could pass while the real check inside the transaction then fails with a \"group is full\" error returned as a plain object `{ error: ..., count }` from the transaction, which the outer code checks with `if (result.error)` — that path is fine. The same stale read pattern exists in `/apply/route.ts` at lines 43-45. These are minor in practice given the transaction safety net, but any code path that relies on the pre-read `is_full` as a definitive gate is fragile.\n\nMore importantly: in `/join/route.ts` line 34:\n\n```ts\nif (listing.requires_approval) {\n```\n\n`listing` is typed as `Record<string, unknown>`, so `listing.requires_approval` is `unknown`. In JavaScript, `if (unknown)` is truthy for any non-falsy value, but SQLite returns integers (0/1). `if (1)` is truthy, so this accidentally works. However it's a correctness smell worth noting.\n\n---\n\n### 2. `readingPace` filter in the API performs a LIKE match, but the filter UI sends fixed enum values\n\n**Confidence: 85**\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/route.ts`, lines 30-33; `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/page.tsx`, lines 292-297\n\nThe API filter is:\n```ts\nconditions.push(\"l.reading_pace LIKE ?\");\nparams.push(`%${readingPace}%`);\n```\n\nBut the UI sends values like `\"chapter\"`, `\"pages\"`, `\"week\"`, `\"day\"`. Meanwhile the database stores free-text values from the create form (e.g. `\"1 chapter per week\"`, `\"50 pages per day\"`). The LIKE match on `\"chapter\"` will correctly match `\"1 chapter per week\"`, but the filter labelled \"Chapter-based\" in the UI sends `\"chapter\"`, which would also match the word \"chapter\" appearing anywhere in any free-text pace. For \"pages\" it would additionally match strings like \"100 pages per 5 days\", making the filter imprecise and potentially returning false positives across pace categories. The root cause is that `reading_pace` is free text in the DB but treated as a structured enum in the filter. This silently produces wrong filter results for users.\n\n---\n\n### 3. `generateMetadata` in `[id]/page.tsx` calls the DB directly on the server — but `ListingDetail` fetches from the API on the client, causing a split data path with no shared error handling\n\n**Confidence: 82**\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/listings/[id]/page.tsx`, lines 26-34\n\n`generateMetadata` queries the DB synchronously on the server. If the listing exists for metadata generation but is deleted or becomes unavailable between the server render and the client fetch, `ListingDetail` will get a 404 from the API while the server already generated valid OG metadata. More concretely: the server-side metadata query in `generateMetadata` has no try/catch. `db.prepare(...).get(listingId)` can throw (e.g. DB corruption, schema mismatch) and will cause an unhandled exception at metadata generation time, crashing the page with a 500 instead of returning `{ title: \"Listing not found — Bookmate\" }`.\n\nFix — wrap the DB call:\n```ts\ntry {\n  const listing = db.prepare(`...`).get(listingId) as ListingRow | undefined;\n  if (!listing) return { title: \"Listing not found — Bookmate\" };\n  // ... build metadata\n} catch {\n  return { title: \"Listing not found — Bookmate\" };\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### 4. `notifyGroupFull` always uses Telegram-specific language regardless of platform preference\n\n**Confidence: 83**\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/notifications.ts`, lines 118-121\n\n```ts\nconst botConfigured = !!process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN;\nconst message = botConfigured\n  ? `The reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" is now full! The Telegram group will be set up automatically.`\n  : `The reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" is now full! The organizer will share a Telegram link soon.`;\n```\n\n`notifyGroupFull` does not check `listing.platform_preference`. If a group uses Discord (a fully supported platform in this codebase), all members receive a notification saying \"The Telegram group will be set up automatically\" or \"the organizer will share a Telegram link soon.\" This is factually wrong and will confuse Discord-preference group members. The listing's `platform_preference` is available in the same table and should be fetched alongside `book_title`.\n\n---\n\n### 5. The `GET /api/listings` route uses spread syntax `db.prepare().get(...params)` which is non-standard for better-sqlite3\n\n**Confidence: 80**\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/route.ts`, lines 49-53\n\n```ts\nconst countResult = db\n  .prepare(`SELECT COUNT(*) as total FROM listings l WHERE ${whereClause}`)\n  .get(...params) as { total: number };\n```\n\n`better-sqlite3`'s `.get()` and `.all()` accept either a list of positional arguments or a single array — but the spread syntax `...params` only works if `params` has a fixed, small, known arity since there is no rest parameter on the underlying native binding. In practice, `better-sqlite3` does accept a spread of individual values, but the idiomatic and documented form is `.get(params)` (passing the array directly) or `.all(params)`. More importantly, line 67:\n\n```ts\n.all(...params, PAGE_SIZE, offset)\n```\n\nThis appends `PAGE_SIZE` and `offset` *after* the spread. `better-sqlite3`'s `.all()` signature is `all(...bindParameters)` where bind parameters can be an array or individual values. Mixing a spread array with trailing positional args like `...params, PAGE_SIZE, offset` is not documented behaviour and may silently bind incorrectly if `better-sqlite3` interprets the first argument as an array when there are multiple arguments. This should be:\n\n```ts\n.all([...params, PAGE_SIZE, offset])\n```\n\n---\n\n## Important Issues\n\n### 6. Profile page: tab data is re-fetched every time a tab is revisited\n\n**Confidence: 80**\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/profile/page.tsx`, lines 123-127\n\n```ts\nuseEffect(() => {\n  if (activeTab === \"reading\") fetchReading();\n  if (activeTab === \"genres\") fetchGenres();\n  if (activeTab === \"reputation\") fetchReputation();\n}, [activeTab, fetchReading, fetchGenres, fetchReputation]);\n```\n\nEvery time the user switches back to a previously visited tab, a fresh network request fires. There's no guard like \"already loaded.\" For the \"Reading Activity\" and \"Reputation\" tabs this means redundant requests on every tab switch. Given `fetchReading`, `fetchGenres`, and `fetchReputation` are defined with `useCallback` with empty dependency arrays, they are stable — but there's no state tracking whether data has already been fetched. The practical impact is unnecessary server hits and the loading spinner flashing on each revisit, which is poor UX. A simple `hasFetchedReading` boolean ref would fix this.\n\n---\n\n### 7. `handleJoin` and `handleApply` in `ListingDetail.tsx` don't reset `joining`/`applying` state on navigation\n\n**Confidence: 80**\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/listings/[id]/ListingDetail.tsx`, lines 171-191 and 193-213\n\n```ts\nconst handleJoin = async () => {\n  setJoining(true);\n  ...\n  if (res.status === 401) {\n    router.push(\"/auth/login\");\n    return;          // <-- finally block is still executed\n  }\n```\n\nThe `finally` block (`setJoining(false)`) does execute even on the early return because there is no `finally` — actually looking at the code more carefully, there is no `finally` in `handleJoin`/`handleApply`. The `setJoining(false)` is inside the `catch` block and the `finally`. Wait — re-reading: `handleJoin` sets `setJoining(true)` then on 401 does `router.push` and `return`. There is no `finally` in `handleJoin`, so `setJoining` is never reset to `false` on 401 redirect. The button stays in \"Joining...\" state until navigation completes. Similarly `handleApply` has the same pattern. This is a real but minor state leak.\n\nActually on re-read, `handleJoin` does not have a `finally`. The only place `setJoining(false)` is called is in the `catch` — not on the success path or 401 path. This means after a successful join (which calls `await fetchListing()` and then falls through the function), `joining` is never set back to `false`. The button permanently shows \"Joining...\" after a successful join until the component re-renders and replaces the button with a \"Member\" state. Since `fetchListing()` updates `listing.isMember`, the button will re-render to show a different element, so in practice the user won't see the stuck state for long — but it's still a logic error.\n\nConfidence on this being genuinely impactful: 80. The `finally`-less pattern means on any error path that doesn't hit `catch`, loading state leaks.\n\n---\n\n### 8. `requireAuth` returns `null` on failure but callers don't return a 401 response automatically — the name implies middleware behavior it doesn't have\n\n**Confidence: 80**\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/session.ts`, lines 37-43\n\n```ts\nexport async function requireAuth() {\n  const session = await getSession();\n  if (!session.userId) {\n    return null;\n  }\n  return session;\n}\n```\n\n`requireAuth` does not throw or redirect — it returns `null`. Every API route must manually check `if (!session) return NextResponse.json(...)`. This is consistently done across routes reviewed, so it works. However, the function name `requireAuth` implies the \"require\" semantics are enforced by the function itself. If a future developer adds a new route and forgets the null check — a common mistake — the route silently accepts unauthenticated requests. This is a design smell that is one missed check away from a security bug. This is noted as a pattern risk rather than a current bug.\n\n---\n\n## Summary\n\n| # | Issue | Severity | File |\n|---|-------|----------|------|\n| 1 | Stale `is_full` pre-transaction check (defence in depth gap) | Critical | `api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts` |\n| 2 | `readingPace` LIKE filter produces incorrect/imprecise results vs. free-text DB values | Critical | `api/listings/route.ts` + `app/page.tsx` |\n| 3 | `generateMetadata` DB call has no try/catch — can crash page with 500 | Critical | `app/listings/[id]/page.tsx` |\n| 4 | `notifyGroupFull` always emits Telegram-specific language for Discord groups | Critical | `lib/notifications.ts` |\n| 5 | `better-sqlite3` `.all(...params, PAGE_SIZE, offset)` spread + trailing args is non-idiomatic and potentially incorrect | Critical | `api/listings/route.ts` |\n| 6 | Tab data re-fetched on every revisit — loading flash UX + unnecessary requests | Important | `app/profile/page.tsx` |\n| 7 | `joining`/`applying` loading state not reset on 401 redirect path | Important | `app/listings/[id]/ListingDetail.tsx` |\n| 8 | `requireAuth` returning null is a footgun for future security bugs | Important | `lib/session.ts` |"},{"type":"text","text":"agentId: a3d5c06c8cdbedb7f (use SendMessage with to: 'a3d5c06c8cdbedb7f' to continue this agent)\n<usage>total_tokens: 56728\ntool_uses: 22\nduration_ms: 99755</usage>"}]}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"e8f30eef-be0b-49af-a8d3-712787ead3bb","uuid":"3c21f3fb-3ae1-4938-a613-449d5dfa5121","timestamp":"2026-03-23T11:29:38.179Z","tool_use_result":{"status":"completed","prompt":"Review the Bookmate Next.js application at /root/Projects/bookmate/app for bugs, code quality issues, and potential improvements. Focus on:\n\n1. The main pages: app/app/page.tsx, app/app/listings/[id]/page.tsx, app/app/listings/create/page.tsx, app/app/profile/page.tsx\n2. API routes: app/app/api/listings/route.ts, app/app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts\n3. Core libs: app/lib/db.ts, app/lib/session.ts, app/lib/notifications.ts\n\nLook for:\n- Bugs or logic errors\n- Missing error handling\n- Security issues\n- Performance problems\n- UX issues (like missing loading states, confusing behavior)\n- Stale or unused code\n\nOnly report HIGH confidence issues that actually matter. Do NOT report style preferences or minor nits.","agentId":"a3d5c06c8cdbedb7f","content":[{"type":"text","text":"I now have a thorough understanding of all the relevant code. Let me compile the findings.\n\n---\n\n## Code Review: Bookmate Next.js Application\n\nReviewing: `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/page.tsx`, `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/listings/[id]/page.tsx` + `ListingDetail.tsx`, `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/listings/create/page.tsx`, `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/profile/page.tsx`, `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/route.ts`, `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts`, `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/db.ts`, `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/session.ts`, `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/notifications.ts`\n\n---\n\n## Critical Issues\n\n### 1. Race condition: stale `is_full` flag used before transaction in `/join` and `/apply` routes\n\n**Confidence: 92**\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts`, lines 41-43\n\n```ts\nif (listing.is_full) {\n  return NextResponse.json({ error: \"This group is already full\" }, { status: 400 });\n}\n```\n\nThe `is_full` check at line 41 happens *before* the transaction at line 57. The `listing` object was fetched at line 21. Between that fetch and the transaction, another request may have filled the group and set `is_full = 1` — but the pre-transaction check passes because it reads a stale value. The transaction does re-count members and correctly gates the insert, so the actual double-join is prevented. However, the pre-transaction `is_full` guard is still misleading: it could pass while the real check inside the transaction then fails with a \"group is full\" error returned as a plain object `{ error: ..., count }` from the transaction, which the outer code checks with `if (result.error)` — that path is fine. The same stale read pattern exists in `/apply/route.ts` at lines 43-45. These are minor in practice given the transaction safety net, but any code path that relies on the pre-read `is_full` as a definitive gate is fragile.\n\nMore importantly: in `/join/route.ts` line 34:\n\n```ts\nif (listing.requires_approval) {\n```\n\n`listing` is typed as `Record<string, unknown>`, so `listing.requires_approval` is `unknown`. In JavaScript, `if (unknown)` is truthy for any non-falsy value, but SQLite returns integers (0/1). `if (1)` is truthy, so this accidentally works. However it's a correctness smell worth noting.\n\n---\n\n### 2. `readingPace` filter in the API performs a LIKE match, but the filter UI sends fixed enum values\n\n**Confidence: 85**\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/route.ts`, lines 30-33; `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/page.tsx`, lines 292-297\n\nThe API filter is:\n```ts\nconditions.push(\"l.reading_pace LIKE ?\");\nparams.push(`%${readingPace}%`);\n```\n\nBut the UI sends values like `\"chapter\"`, `\"pages\"`, `\"week\"`, `\"day\"`. Meanwhile the database stores free-text values from the create form (e.g. `\"1 chapter per week\"`, `\"50 pages per day\"`). The LIKE match on `\"chapter\"` will correctly match `\"1 chapter per week\"`, but the filter labelled \"Chapter-based\" in the UI sends `\"chapter\"`, which would also match the word \"chapter\" appearing anywhere in any free-text pace. For \"pages\" it would additionally match strings like \"100 pages per 5 days\", making the filter imprecise and potentially returning false positives across pace categories. The root cause is that `reading_pace` is free text in the DB but treated as a structured enum in the filter. This silently produces wrong filter results for users.\n\n---\n\n### 3. `generateMetadata` in `[id]/page.tsx` calls the DB directly on the server — but `ListingDetail` fetches from the API on the client, causing a split data path with no shared error handling\n\n**Confidence: 82**\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/listings/[id]/page.tsx`, lines 26-34\n\n`generateMetadata` queries the DB synchronously on the server. If the listing exists for metadata generation but is deleted or becomes unavailable between the server render and the client fetch, `ListingDetail` will get a 404 from the API while the server already generated valid OG metadata. More concretely: the server-side metadata query in `generateMetadata` has no try/catch. `db.prepare(...).get(listingId)` can throw (e.g. DB corruption, schema mismatch) and will cause an unhandled exception at metadata generation time, crashing the page with a 500 instead of returning `{ title: \"Listing not found — Bookmate\" }`.\n\nFix — wrap the DB call:\n```ts\ntry {\n  const listing = db.prepare(`...`).get(listingId) as ListingRow | undefined;\n  if (!listing) return { title: \"Listing not found — Bookmate\" };\n  // ... build metadata\n} catch {\n  return { title: \"Listing not found — Bookmate\" };\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### 4. `notifyGroupFull` always uses Telegram-specific language regardless of platform preference\n\n**Confidence: 83**\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/notifications.ts`, lines 118-121\n\n```ts\nconst botConfigured = !!process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN;\nconst message = botConfigured\n  ? `The reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" is now full! The Telegram group will be set up automatically.`\n  : `The reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" is now full! The organizer will share a Telegram link soon.`;\n```\n\n`notifyGroupFull` does not check `listing.platform_preference`. If a group uses Discord (a fully supported platform in this codebase), all members receive a notification saying \"The Telegram group will be set up automatically\" or \"the organizer will share a Telegram link soon.\" This is factually wrong and will confuse Discord-preference group members. The listing's `platform_preference` is available in the same table and should be fetched alongside `book_title`.\n\n---\n\n### 5. The `GET /api/listings` route uses spread syntax `db.prepare().get(...params)` which is non-standard for better-sqlite3\n\n**Confidence: 80**\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/route.ts`, lines 49-53\n\n```ts\nconst countResult = db\n  .prepare(`SELECT COUNT(*) as total FROM listings l WHERE ${whereClause}`)\n  .get(...params) as { total: number };\n```\n\n`better-sqlite3`'s `.get()` and `.all()` accept either a list of positional arguments or a single array — but the spread syntax `...params` only works if `params` has a fixed, small, known arity since there is no rest parameter on the underlying native binding. In practice, `better-sqlite3` does accept a spread of individual values, but the idiomatic and documented form is `.get(params)` (passing the array directly) or `.all(params)`. More importantly, line 67:\n\n```ts\n.all(...params, PAGE_SIZE, offset)\n```\n\nThis appends `PAGE_SIZE` and `offset` *after* the spread. `better-sqlite3`'s `.all()` signature is `all(...bindParameters)` where bind parameters can be an array or individual values. Mixing a spread array with trailing positional args like `...params, PAGE_SIZE, offset` is not documented behaviour and may silently bind incorrectly if `better-sqlite3` interprets the first argument as an array when there are multiple arguments. This should be:\n\n```ts\n.all([...params, PAGE_SIZE, offset])\n```\n\n---\n\n## Important Issues\n\n### 6. Profile page: tab data is re-fetched every time a tab is revisited\n\n**Confidence: 80**\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/profile/page.tsx`, lines 123-127\n\n```ts\nuseEffect(() => {\n  if (activeTab === \"reading\") fetchReading();\n  if (activeTab === \"genres\") fetchGenres();\n  if (activeTab === \"reputation\") fetchReputation();\n}, [activeTab, fetchReading, fetchGenres, fetchReputation]);\n```\n\nEvery time the user switches back to a previously visited tab, a fresh network request fires. There's no guard like \"already loaded.\" For the \"Reading Activity\" and \"Reputation\" tabs this means redundant requests on every tab switch. Given `fetchReading`, `fetchGenres`, and `fetchReputation` are defined with `useCallback` with empty dependency arrays, they are stable — but there's no state tracking whether data has already been fetched. The practical impact is unnecessary server hits and the loading spinner flashing on each revisit, which is poor UX. A simple `hasFetchedReading` boolean ref would fix this.\n\n---\n\n### 7. `handleJoin` and `handleApply` in `ListingDetail.tsx` don't reset `joining`/`applying` state on navigation\n\n**Confidence: 80**\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/listings/[id]/ListingDetail.tsx`, lines 171-191 and 193-213\n\n```ts\nconst handleJoin = async () => {\n  setJoining(true);\n  ...\n  if (res.status === 401) {\n    router.push(\"/auth/login\");\n    return;          // <-- finally block is still executed\n  }\n```\n\nThe `finally` block (`setJoining(false)`) does execute even on the early return because there is no `finally` — actually looking at the code more carefully, there is no `finally` in `handleJoin`/`handleApply`. The `setJoining(false)` is inside the `catch` block and the `finally`. Wait — re-reading: `handleJoin` sets `setJoining(true)` then on 401 does `router.push` and `return`. There is no `finally` in `handleJoin`, so `setJoining` is never reset to `false` on 401 redirect. The button stays in \"Joining...\" state until navigation completes. Similarly `handleApply` has the same pattern. This is a real but minor state leak.\n\nActually on re-read, `handleJoin` does not have a `finally`. The only place `setJoining(false)` is called is in the `catch` — not on the success path or 401 path. This means after a successful join (which calls `await fetchListing()` and then falls through the function), `joining` is never set back to `false`. The button permanently shows \"Joining...\" after a successful join until the component re-renders and replaces the button with a \"Member\" state. Since `fetchListing()` updates `listing.isMember`, the button will re-render to show a different element, so in practice the user won't see the stuck state for long — but it's still a logic error.\n\nConfidence on this being genuinely impactful: 80. The `finally`-less pattern means on any error path that doesn't hit `catch`, loading state leaks.\n\n---\n\n### 8. `requireAuth` returns `null` on failure but callers don't return a 401 response automatically — the name implies middleware behavior it doesn't have\n\n**Confidence: 80**\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/session.ts`, lines 37-43\n\n```ts\nexport async function requireAuth() {\n  const session = await getSession();\n  if (!session.userId) {\n    return null;\n  }\n  return session;\n}\n```\n\n`requireAuth` does not throw or redirect — it returns `null`. Every API route must manually check `if (!session) return NextResponse.json(...)`. This is consistently done across routes reviewed, so it works. However, the function name `requireAuth` implies the \"require\" semantics are enforced by the function itself. If a future developer adds a new route and forgets the null check — a common mistake — the route silently accepts unauthenticated requests. This is a design smell that is one missed check away from a security bug. This is noted as a pattern risk rather than a current bug.\n\n---\n\n## Summary\n\n| # | Issue | Severity | File |\n|---|-------|----------|------|\n| 1 | Stale `is_full` pre-transaction check (defence in depth gap) | Critical | `api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts` |\n| 2 | `readingPace` LIKE filter produces incorrect/imprecise results vs. free-text DB values | Critical | `api/listings/route.ts` + `app/page.tsx` |\n| 3 | `generateMetadata` DB call has no try/catch — can crash page with 500 | Critical | `app/listings/[id]/page.tsx` |\n| 4 | `notifyGroupFull` always emits Telegram-specific language for Discord groups | Critical | `lib/notifications.ts` |\n| 5 | `better-sqlite3` `.all(...params, PAGE_SIZE, offset)` spread + trailing args is non-idiomatic and potentially incorrect | Critical | `api/listings/route.ts` |\n| 6 | Tab data re-fetched on every revisit — loading flash UX + unnecessary requests | Important | `app/profile/page.tsx` |\n| 7 | `joining`/`applying` loading state not reset on 401 redirect path | Important | `app/listings/[id]/ListingDetail.tsx` |\n| 8 | `requireAuth` returning null is a footgun for future security bugs | Important | `lib/session.ts` |"}],"totalDurationMs":99755,"totalTokens":56728,"totalToolUseCount":22,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":2453,"cache_read_input_tokens":50893,"output_tokens":3381,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":2453},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01CPfZCHx85HfGVXjWjUxNLP","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import type { Metadata } from \"next\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import ListingDetail from \"./ListingDetail\";\n     4→\n     5→interface ListingRow {\n     6→  book_title: string;\n     7→  book_author: string;\n     8→  book_cover_url: string;\n     9→  reading_pace: string;\n    10→  author_name: string;\n    11→  max_group_size: number;\n    12→}\n    13→\n    14→export async function generateMetadata({\n    15→  params,\n    16→}: {\n    17→  params: Promise<{ id: string }>;\n    18→}): Promise<Metadata> {\n    19→  const { id } = await params;\n    20→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    21→\n    22→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    23→    return { title: \"Listing not found — Bookmate\" };\n    24→  }\n    25→\n    26→  const listing = db\n    27→    .prepare(\n    28→      `SELECT l.book_title, l.book_author, l.book_cover_url, l.reading_pace,\n    29→              l.max_group_size, u.display_name as author_name\n    30→       FROM listings l\n    31→       JOIN users u ON l.author_id = u.id\n    32→       WHERE l.id = ?`\n    33→    )\n    34→    .get(listingId) as ListingRow | undefined;\n    35→\n    36→  if (!listing) {\n    37→    return { title: \"Listing not found — Bookmate\" };\n    38→  }\n    39→\n    40→  const title = `${listing.book_title} by ${listing.book_author} — Bookmate`;\n    41→  const description = `Join a reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" by ${listing.book_author}. Pace: ${listing.reading_pace}. Up to ${listing.max_group_size} readers. Posted by ${listing.author_name}.`;\n    42→\n    43→  return {\n    44→    title,\n    45→    description,\n    46→    openGraph: {\n    47→      title,\n    48→      description,\n    49→      type: \"article\",\n    50→      ...(listing.book_cover_url\n    51→        ? {\n    52→            images: [\n    53→              {\n    54→                url: listing.book_cover_url,\n    55→                alt: `Cover of ${listing.book_title}`,\n    56→              },\n    57→            ],\n    58→          }\n    59→        : {}),\n    60→    },\n    61→    twitter: {\n    62→      card: listing.book_cover_url ? \"summary_large_image\" : \"summary\",\n    63→      title,\n    64→      description,\n    65→      ...(listing.book_cover_url ? { images: [listing.book_cover_url] } : {}),\n    66→    },\n    67→  };\n    68→}\n    69→\n    70→export default function ListingPage() {\n    71→  return <ListingDetail />;\n    72→}\n    73→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"e8f30eef-be0b-49af-a8d3-712787ead3bb","uuid":"f9412980-caab-498a-a43c-b1ed719507b6","timestamp":"2026-03-23T11:29:52.509Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/listings/[id]/page.tsx","content":"import type { Metadata } from \"next\";\nimport db from \"@/lib/db\";\nimport ListingDetail from \"./ListingDetail\";\n\ninterface ListingRow {\n  book_title: string;\n  book_author: string;\n  book_cover_url: string;\n  reading_pace: string;\n  author_name: string;\n  max_group_size: number;\n}\n\nexport async function generateMetadata({\n  params,\n}: {\n  params: Promise<{ id: string }>;\n}): Promise<Metadata> {\n  const { id } = await params;\n  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n\n  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    return { title: \"Listing not found — Bookmate\" };\n  }\n\n  const listing = db\n    .prepare(\n      `SELECT l.book_title, l.book_author, l.book_cover_url, l.reading_pace,\n              l.max_group_size, u.display_name as author_name\n       FROM listings l\n       JOIN users u ON l.author_id = u.id\n       WHERE l.id = ?`\n    )\n    .get(listingId) as ListingRow | undefined;\n\n  if (!listing) {\n    return { title: \"Listing not found — Bookmate\" };\n  }\n\n  const title = `${listing.book_title} by ${listing.book_author} — Bookmate`;\n  const description = `Join a reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" by ${listing.book_author}. Pace: ${listing.reading_pace}. Up to ${listing.max_group_size} readers. Posted by ${listing.author_name}.`;\n\n  return {\n    title,\n    description,\n    openGraph: {\n      title,\n      description,\n      type: \"article\",\n      ...(listing.book_cover_url\n        ? {\n            images: [\n              {\n                url: listing.book_cover_url,\n                alt: `Cover of ${listing.book_title}`,\n              },\n            ],\n          }\n        : {}),\n    },\n    twitter: {\n      card: listing.book_cover_url ? \"summary_large_image\" : \"summary\",\n      title,\n      description,\n      ...(listing.book_cover_url ? { images: [listing.book_cover_url] } : {}),\n    },\n  };\n}\n\nexport default function ListingPage() {\n  return <ListingDetail />;\n}\n","numLines":73,"startLine":1,"totalLines":73}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_016azwDsAcYMg4P2wAh117zj","type":"tool_result","content":"   100→    const type = decision === \"approved\" ? \"application_approved\" : \"application_rejected\";\n   101→    const message =\n   102→      decision === \"approved\"\n   103→        ? `Your application to join \"${listing.book_title}\" was approved!`\n   104→        : `Your application to join \"${listing.book_title}\" was not accepted.`;\n   105→    createNotification(userId, listingId, type, message);\n   106→  }\n   107→}\n   108→\n   109→export function notifyGroupFull(listingId: number) {\n   110→  const listing = db\n   111→    .prepare(\"SELECT book_title FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n   112→    .get(listingId) as { book_title: string } | undefined;\n   113→  const members = db\n   114→    .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n   115→    .all(listingId) as { user_id: number }[];\n   116→\n   117→  if (listing) {\n   118→    const botConfigured = !!process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN;\n   119→    const message = botConfigured\n   120→      ? `The reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" is now full! The Telegram group will be set up automatically.`\n   121→      : `The reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" is now full! The organizer will share a Telegram link soon.`;\n   122→\n   123→    for (const member of members) {\n   124→      createNotification(\n   125→        member.user_id,\n   126→        listingId,\n   127→        \"group_full\",\n   128→        message\n   129→      );\n   130→    }\n   131→  }\n   132→}\n   133→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"e8f30eef-be0b-49af-a8d3-712787ead3bb","uuid":"50ee9db9-0dd9-4552-a064-0d1f9d5e32eb","timestamp":"2026-03-23T11:29:53.391Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/notifications.ts","content":"    const type = decision === \"approved\" ? \"application_approved\" : \"application_rejected\";\n    const message =\n      decision === \"approved\"\n        ? `Your application to join \"${listing.book_title}\" was approved!`\n        : `Your application to join \"${listing.book_title}\" was not accepted.`;\n    createNotification(userId, listingId, type, message);\n  }\n}\n\nexport function notifyGroupFull(listingId: number) {\n  const listing = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT book_title FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    .get(listingId) as { book_title: string } | undefined;\n  const members = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n    .all(listingId) as { user_id: number }[];\n\n  if (listing) {\n    const botConfigured = !!process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN;\n    const message = botConfigured\n      ? `The reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" is now full! The Telegram group will be set up automatically.`\n      : `The reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" is now full! The organizer will share a Telegram link soon.`;\n\n    for (const member of members) {\n      createNotification(\n        member.user_id,\n        listingId,\n        \"group_full\",\n        message\n      );\n    }\n  }\n}\n","numLines":34,"startLine":100,"totalLines":133}}}

Please try again.\");\n   232→    } finally {\n   233→      setDecidingId(null);\n   234→    }\n   235→  };\n   236→\n   237→  const isValidTelegramLink = (link: string) => {\n   238→    return /^https:\\/\\/t\\.me\\/[\\w+/]+$/.test(link.trim());\n   239→  };"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"e8f30eef-be0b-49af-a8d3-712787ead3bb","uuid":"2a52de1d-1881-4547-b2b4-4e90ce987e7f","timestamp":"2026-03-23T11:29:54.237Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/listings/[id]/ListingDetail.tsx","content":"          ...prev,\n          [memberId]: { score, comment: ratingComments[memberId] || \"\" },\n        }));\n      }\n    } catch {\n      setRatingMessage(\"Failed to submit rating\");\n    } finally {\n      setRatingSaving(null);\n    }\n  };\n\n  const handleJoin = async () => {\n    setJoining(true);\n    setError(\"\");\n    try {\n      const res = await fetch(`/api/listings/${id}/join`, { method: \"POST\" });\n      const data = await res.json();\n      if (!res.ok) {\n        if (res.status === 401) {\n          router.push(\"/auth/login\");\n          return;\n        }\n        setError(data.error);\n        return;\n      }\n      await fetchListing();\n    } catch {\n      setError(\"Failed to join. Please try again.\");\n    } finally {\n      setJoining(false);\n    }\n  };\n\n  const handleApply = async () => {\n    setApplying(true);\n    setError(\"\");\n    try {\n      const res = await fetch(`/api/listings/${id}/apply`, { method: \"POST\" });\n      const data = await res.json();\n      if (!res.ok) {\n        if (res.status === 401) {\n          router.push(\"/auth/login\");\n          return;\n        }\n        setError(data.error);\n        return;\n      }\n      await fetchListing();\n    } catch {\n      setError(\"Failed to apply. Please try again.\");\n    } finally {\n      setApplying(false);\n    }\n  };\n\n  const handleDecision = async (applicationId: number, action: \"approve\" | \"reject\") => {\n    setDecidingId(applicationId);\n    setError(\"\");\n    try {\n      const res = await fetch(`/api/listings/${id}/applications`, {\n        method: \"PATCH\",\n        headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n        body: JSON.stringify({ applicationId, action }),\n      });\n      const data = await res.json();\n      if (!res.ok) {\n        setError(data.error);\n        return;\n      }\n      await fetchListing();\n    } catch {\n      setError(\"Failed to process application. Please try again.\");\n    } finally {\n      setDecidingId(null);\n    }\n  };\n\n  const isValidTelegramLink = (link: string) => {\n    return /^https:\\/\\/t\\.me\\/[\\w+/]+$/.test(link.trim());\n  };","numLines":80,"startLine":160,"totalLines":1352}}}

useCallback(() => {\n   113→    setReputationLoading(true);\n   114→    fetch(\"/api/profile/reputation\")\n   115→      .then((r) => r.json())\n   116→      .then((data) => {\n   117→        setReputation(data.reputation || null);\n   118→        setReputationLoading(false);\n   119→      })\n   120→      .catch(() => setReputationLoading(false));\n   121→  }, []);\n   122→\n   123→  useEffect(() => {\n   124→    if (activeTab === \"reading\") fetchReading();\n   125→    if (activeTab === \"genres\") fetchGenres();\n   126→    if (activeTab === \"reputation\") fetchReputation();\n   127→  }, [activeTab, fetchReading, fetchGenres, fetchReputation]);\n   128→\n   129→  const handleSave = async (e: React.FormEvent) => {\n   130→    e.preventDefault();\n   131→    setSaving(true);\n   132→    setMessage(\"\");\n   133→\n   134→    try {\n   135→      const res = await fetch(\"/api/profile\", {\n   136→        method: \"PATCH\",\n   137→        headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n   138→        body: JSON.stringify({ displayName, bio }),\n   139→      });\n   140→      const data = await res.json();\n   141→      if (res.ok) {\n   142→        setMessage(\"Profile updated!\");\n   143→      } else {\n   144→        setMessage(data.error || \"Failed to update\");\n   145→      }\n   146→    } catch {\n   147→      setMessage(\"Something went wrong\");\n   148→    } finally {\n   149→      setSaving(false);"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"e8f30eef-be0b-49af-a8d3-712787ead3bb","uuid":"8ab66964-94d1-415d-919e-5df77a527830","timestamp":"2026-03-23T11:29:54.698Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/profile/page.tsx","content":"  }, []);\n\n  const fetchReputation = useCallback(() => {\n    setReputationLoading(true);\n    fetch(\"/api/profile/reputation\")\n      .then((r) => r.json())\n      .then((data) => {\n        setReputation(data.reputation || null);\n        setReputationLoading(false);\n      })\n      .catch(() => setReputationLoading(false));\n  }, []);\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    if (activeTab === \"reading\") fetchReading();\n    if (activeTab === \"genres\") fetchGenres();\n    if (activeTab === \"reputation\") fetchReputation();\n  }, [activeTab, fetchReading, fetchGenres, fetchReputation]);\n\n  const handleSave = async (e: React.FormEvent) => {\n    e.preventDefault();\n    setSaving(true);\n    setMessage(\"\");\n\n    try {\n      const res = await fetch(\"/api/profile\", {\n        method: \"PATCH\",\n        headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n        body: JSON.stringify({ displayName, bio }),\n      });\n      const data = await res.json();\n      if (res.ok) {\n        setMessage(\"Profile updated!\");\n      } else {\n        setMessage(data.error || \"Failed to update\");\n      }\n    } catch {\n      setMessage(\"Something went wrong\");\n    } finally {\n      setSaving(false);","numLines":40,"startLine":110,"totalLines":735}}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01E4dUiktB2ag7h7YczBXGzx","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_014UX9vizXaYD4gA3tduauwB","name":"Edit","input":{"replace_all":false,"file_path":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/listings/[id]/page.tsx","old_string":"  const listing = db\n    .prepare(\n      `SELECT l.book_title, l.book_author, l.book_cover_url, l.reading_pace,\n              l.max_group_size, u.display_name as author_name\n       FROM listings l\n       JOIN users u ON l.author_id = u.id\n       WHERE l.id = ?`\n    )\n    .get(listingId) as ListingRow | undefined;\n\n  if (!listing) {\n    return { title: \"Listing not found — Bookmate\" };\n  }\n\n  const title = `${listing.book_title} by ${listing.book_author} — Bookmate`;\n  const description = `Join a reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" by ${listing.book_author}. Pace: ${listing.reading_pace}. Up to ${listing.max_group_size} readers. Posted by ${listing.author_name}.`;\n\n  return {\n    title,\n    description,\n    openGraph: {\n      title,\n      description,\n      type: \"article\",\n      ...(listing.book_cover_url\n        ? {\n            images: [\n              {\n                url: listing.book_cover_url,\n                alt: `Cover of ${listing.book_title}`,\n              },\n            ],\n          }\n        : {}),\n    },\n    twitter: {\n      card: listing.book_cover_url ? \"summary_large_image\" : \"summary\",\n      title,\n      description,\n      ...(listing.book_cover_url ? { images: [listing.book_cover_url] } : {}),\n    },\n  };","new_string":"  try {\n    const listing = db\n      .prepare(\n        `SELECT l.book_title, l.book_author, l.book_cover_url, l.reading_pace,\n                l.max_group_size, u.display_name as author_name\n         FROM listings l\n         JOIN users u ON l.author_id = u.id\n         WHERE l.id = ?`\n      )\n      .get(listingId) as ListingRow | undefined;\n\n    if (!listing) {\n      return { title: \"Listing not found — Bookmate\" };\n    }\n\n    const title = `${listing.book_title} by ${listing.book_author} — Bookmate`;\n    const description = `Join a reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" by ${listing.book_author}. Pace: ${listing.reading_pace}. Up to ${listing.max_group_size} readers. Posted by ${listing.author_name}.`;\n\n    return {\n      title,\n      description,\n      openGraph: {\n        title,\n        description,\n        type: \"article\",\n        ...(listing.book_cover_url\n          ? {\n              images: [\n                {\n                  url: listing.book_cover_url,\n                  alt: `Cover of ${listing.book_title}`,\n                },\n              ],\n            }\n          : {}),\n      },\n      twitter: {\n        card: listing.book_cover_url ? \"summary_large_image\" : \"summary\",\n        title,\n        description,\n        ...(listing.book_cover_url ? { images: [listing.book_cover_url] } : {}),\n      },\n    };\n  } catch {\n    return { title: \"Listing — Bookmate\" };\n  }"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":3160,"cache_read_input_tokens":46328,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":3160},"output_tokens":8,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"e8f30eef-be0b-49af-a8d3-712787ead3bb","uuid":"9723ed8b-4252-466c-ac34-b2af62710c5a"}

Pace: ${listing.reading_pace}. Up to ${listing.max_group_size} readers. Posted by ${listing.author_name}.`;\n\n  return {\n    title,\n    description,\n    openGraph: {\n      title,\n      description,\n      type: \"article\",\n      ...(listing.book_cover_url\n        ? {\n            images: [\n              {\n                url: listing.book_cover_url,\n                alt: `Cover of ${listing.book_title}`,\n              },\n            ],\n          }\n        : {}),\n    },\n    twitter: {\n      card: listing.book_cover_url ? \"summary_large_image\" : \"summary\",\n      title,\n      description,\n      ...(listing.book_cover_url ? { images: [listing.book_cover_url] } : {}),\n    },\n  };","newString":"  try {\n    const listing = db\n      .prepare(\n        `SELECT l.book_title, l.book_author, l.book_cover_url, l.reading_pace,\n                l.max_group_size, u.display_name as author_name\n         FROM listings l\n         JOIN users u ON l.author_id = u.id\n         WHERE l.id = ?`\n      )\n      .get(listingId) as ListingRow | undefined;\n\n    if (!listing) {\n      return { title: \"Listing not found — Bookmate\" };\n    }\n\n    const title = `${listing.book_title} by ${listing.book_author} — Bookmate`;\n    const description = `Join a reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" by ${listing.book_author}. Pace: ${listing.reading_pace}. Up to ${listing.max_group_size} readers. Posted by ${listing.author_name}.`;\n\n    return {\n      title,\n      description,\n      openGraph: {\n        title,\n        description,\n        type: \"article\",\n        ...(listing.book_cover_url\n          ? {\n              images: [\n                {\n                  url: listing.book_cover_url,\n                  alt: `Cover of ${listing.book_title}`,\n                },\n              ],\n            }\n          : {}),\n      },\n      twitter: {\n        card: listing.book_cover_url ? \"summary_large_image\" : \"summary\",\n        title,\n        description,\n        ...(listing.book_cover_url ? { images: [listing.book_cover_url] } : {}),\n      },\n    };\n  } catch {\n    return { title: \"Listing — Bookmate\" };\n  }","originalFile":"import type { Metadata } from \"next\";\nimport db from \"@/lib/db\";\nimport ListingDetail from \"./ListingDetail\";\n\ninterface ListingRow {\n  book_title: string;\n  book_author: string;\n  book_cover_url: string;\n  reading_pace: string;\n  author_name: string;\n  max_group_size: number;\n}\n\nexport async function generateMetadata({\n  params,\n}: {\n  params: Promise<{ id: string }>;\n}): Promise<Metadata> {\n  const { id } = await params;\n  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n\n  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    return { title: \"Listing not found — Bookmate\" };\n  }\n\n  const listing = db\n    .prepare(\n      `SELECT l.book_title, l.book_author, l.book_cover_url, l.reading_pace,\n              l.max_group_size, u.display_name as author_name\n       FROM listings l\n       JOIN users u ON l.author_id = u.id\n       WHERE l.id = ?`\n    )\n    .get(listingId) as ListingRow | undefined;\n\n  if (!listing) {\n    return { title: \"Listing not found — Bookmate\" };\n  }\n\n  const title = `${listing.book_title} by ${listing.book_author} — Bookmate`;\n  const description = `Join a reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" by ${listing.book_author}. Pace: ${listing.reading_pace}. Up to ${listing.max_group_size} readers. Posted by ${listing.author_name}.`;\n\n  return {\n    title,\n    description,\n    openGraph: {\n      title,\n      description,\n      type: \"article\",\n      ...(listing.book_cover_url\n        ? {\n            images: [\n              {\n                url: listing.book_cover_url,\n                alt: `Cover of ${listing.book_title}`,\n              },\n            ],\n          }\n        : {}),\n    },\n    twitter: {\n      card: listing.book_cover_url ? \"summary_large_image\" : \"summary\",\n      title,\n      description,\n      ...(listing.book_cover_url ? { images: [listing.book_cover_url] } : {}),\n    },\n  };\n}\n\nexport default function ListingPage() {\n  return <ListingDetail />;\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":23,"oldLines":48,"newStart":23,"newLines":52,"lines":["     return { title: \"Listing not found — Bookmate\" };","   }"," ","-  const listing = db","-    .prepare(","-      `SELECT l.book_title, l.book_author, l.book_cover_url, l.reading_pace,","-              l.max_group_size, u.display_name as author_name","-       FROM listings l","-       JOIN users u ON l.author_id = u.id","-       WHERE l.id = ?`","-    )","-    .get(listingId) as ListingRow | undefined;","+  try {","+    const listing = db","+      .prepare(","+        `SELECT l.book_title, l.book_author, l.book_cover_url, l.reading_pace,","+                l.max_group_size, u.display_name as author_name","+         FROM listings l","+         JOIN users u ON l.author_id = u.id","+         WHERE l.id = ?`","+      )","+      .get(listingId) as ListingRow | undefined;"," ","-  if (!listing) {","-    return { title: \"Listing not found — Bookmate\" };","-  }","+    if (!listing) {","+      return { title: \"Listing not found — Bookmate\" };","+    }"," ","-  const title = `${listing.book_title} by ${listing.book_author} — Bookmate`;","-  const description = `Join a reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" by ${listing.book_author}. Posted by ${listing.author_name}.`;","+    const title = `${listing.book_title} by ${listing.book_author} — Bookmate`;","+    const description = `Join a reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" by ${listing.book_author}. Pace: ${listing.reading_pace}. Up to ${listing.max_group_size} readers. Posted by ${listing.author_name}.`;"," ","-  return {","-    title,","-    description,","-    openGraph: {","+    return {","       title,","       description,","-      type: \"article\",","-      ...(listing.book_cover_url","-        ? {","-            images: [","-              {","-                url: listing.book_cover_url,","-                alt: `Cover of ${listing.book_title}`,","-              },","-            ],","-          }","-        : {}),","-    },","-    twitter: {","-      card: listing.book_cover_url ? \"summary_large_image\" : \"summary\",","-      title,","-      description,","-      ...(listing.book_cover_url ? { images: [listing.book_cover_url] } : {}),","-    },","-  };","+      openGraph: {","+        title,","+        description,","+        type: \"article\",","+        ...(listing.book_cover_url","+          ? {","+              images: [","+                {","+                  url: listing.book_cover_url,","+                  alt: `Cover of ${listing.book_title}`,","+                },","+              ],","+            }","+          : {}),","+      },","+      twitter: {","+        card: listing.book_cover_url ? \"summary_large_image\" : \"summary\",","+        title,","+        description,","+        ...(listing.book_cover_url ? { images: [listing.book_cover_url] } : {}),","+      },","+    };","+  } catch {","+    return { title: \"Listing — Bookmate\" };","+  }"," }"," "," export default function ListingPage() {"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}
